The bug is that validation at lines 967 and 1083 still uses the hardcoded constant.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/020-mcp-working-memory-hybrid-rag/scratch/opencode-working-memory/README.md:116:The plugin exposes these tools to your OpenCode agent:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/020-mcp-working-memory-hybrid-rag/scratch/opencode-working-memory/README.md:164:**Without this plugin:**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/020-mcp-working-memory-hybrid-rag/scratch/opencode-working-memory/README.md:171:**With this plugin:**
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/020-mcp-working-memory-hybrid-rag/scratch/opencode-working-memory/README.md:181:The plugin works great with zero configuration. To customize behavior, modify the constants at the top of `index.ts`. See the [Configuration Guide](docs/configuration.md) for all tunable options.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/020-mcp-working-memory-hybrid-rag/scratch/opencode-working-memory/README.md:187:- `@opencode-ai/plugin` >= 1.2.0
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/001-fix-command-dispatch/z_archive/049-system-analysis-bugs/spec.md:33:**Note:** Profile infrastructure already exists (`resolveDatabasePath()`, `getEmbeddingProfile()`, `profile.getDatabasePath()`). The bug is that validation still uses the hardcoded constant instead of the profile dimension.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:26:The repository's strongest source-confirmed differentiators are (1) the unified `ScanResult` model in `external/src/index.ts:75-175` that branches late into static artifacts, MCP queries, profile files, and blast-radius analysis from one analysis pass; (2) the per-tool profile overlay system in `external/src/generators/ai-config.ts:164-264` that gives Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Copilot, and Windsurf meaningfully different instructions on top of one shared summary; (3) the genuine AST decorator-walking NestJS route detector in `external/src/ast/extract-routes.ts:157-248`; and (4) a small but real precision/recall/F1 fixture harness in `external/src/eval.ts:43-244`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:30:For `Code_Environment/Public`, the highest-leverage adoptions are the orchestration shape (one canonical scan, late-bound projections), the AST-first / regex-fallback / `confidence` label pattern, the per-tool profile overlay split, and the F1 fixture harness as a regression-test pattern. The biggest "do not duplicate" boundary is the cached MCP query interface, which belongs to phase 003-contextador, and the dependency-graph community detection layer, which belongs to phase 004-graphify.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:38:> Research the external repository at `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/external` and identify concrete improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around AST-based codebase analysis, framework/ORM detector architecture, AI-assistant context file generation, MCP tool design, and per-tool config profile patterns.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:44:- Per-tool AI assistant profile generation in `external/src/generators/ai-config.ts`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:106:        +-- --profile <tool>      -> generateProfileConfig(result, root, tool)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:128:- `external/src/generators/ai-config.ts:1-264` — `generateAIConfigs` (5 files) + `generateProfileConfig` (5 profile cases)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:144:- **What it does:** `scan()` performs the core pipeline once: project detection → file collection → 7 parallel detectors → contract enrichment → `writeOutput` (twice, for placeholder and accurate token stats). `main()` then layers optional modes (JSON, HTML, benchmark, profile, blast-radius, MCP, eval, watch) on top of the same `result` object.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:153:- **What it does:** Both functions run `detectProject` + `collectFiles` + 7 parallel detectors + `enrichRouteContracts`. Differences: `scan()` respects `disableDetectors` config, runs plugins, prints CLI progress, calls `writeOutput` twice. `getScanResult()` uses fixed `maxDepth=10`, skips plugins, calls `writeOutput` once, caches the result. There is no shared helper.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:154:- **Why it matters:** Cautionary tale for `Code_Environment/Public`: factor any analogous pipeline into a single library function so toggles like disabled detectors and plugin hooks are not silently divergent across CLI and MCP surfaces.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:294:- **What it does:** Builds a compact ~1-2k token summary (stack, monorepo workspaces, route/model/env counts, top API areas, top 5 hot files, required env vars), then `switch (profile)`:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:301:- **Why it matters:** The single most directly transferable pattern in Codesight. Public could borrow the "shared summary + per-tool instruction overlay" split for `CLAUDE.md` / `.codex` / `.cursor` / `.gemini` directories. Cargo-cult risk: the Claude profile hardcodes `codesight_get_*` MCP tool names, which would only make sense after a similar MCP surface exists.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:304:- **Affected area:** AI-assistant context generation, per-tool config profiles
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:407:<!-- ANCHOR:per-tool-profile-diff -->
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:420:**Pattern to lift for `Code_Environment/Public`:** "Shared canonical summary + per-tool instruction overlay" with a `switch` over tool name and a small `summaryLines` array. Do NOT lift the hardcoded `codesight_get_*` MCP tool names from the Claude profile until an analogous MCP surface exists locally.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:422:<!-- /ANCHOR:per-tool-profile-diff -->
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:469:1. **Single-canonical-`ScanResult` orchestration shape.** Project one analysis result into multiple consumer surfaces (static markdown, MCP, profile files, blast-radius), branching late inside `main()` rather than running parallel pipelines. Anchor: `external/src/index.ts:75-175`. Public lift: define a shared `RepoFingerprint` (or similar) that any context-producing surface — CLAUDE.md generator, MCP tool, code-graph snapshot, spec-kit memory bootstrap — consumes from a single function. F-ORCH-1, F-ORCH-2.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:473:3. **Per-tool profile overlay split.** One shared canonical summary plus a `switch (profile)` of tool-specific instruction blocks. Anchor: `external/src/generators/ai-config.ts:164-264`. Public lift: build a `generateAssistantConfig(repoFingerprint, profile)` that emits CLAUDE.md / .codex / .gemini / .cursorrules with shared summary + tool-specific overlays. Do not hardcode MCP tool names that don't exist locally yet. F-PROFILE-2.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:493:2. **Per-tool profile generation in assistant directories.** The `generateAIConfigs` shared-summary writer is portable, but only `CLAUDE.md` has true upsert semantics in Codesight; the others would clobber existing files. Public must add marker comments / namespaced sections / `Code_Environment/Public/.claude/`-style directory namespacing before adopting. Anchor: `external/src/generators/ai-config.ts:71-158`. F-PROFILE-1.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:544:3. **Plugin API surface and contract.** Iteration 1 noted that `scan()` runs `userConfig.plugins` post-processors but the plugin contract was not explored. If Public adopts a plugin pattern, this is a small follow-up read of `external/src/index.ts:402-413` and the plugin type definitions in `external/src/types.ts`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:545:4. **Token-stat methodology.** `calculateTokenStats(...)` produces the `tokenStats.saved` value used in the Claude profile motivation message but was not traced. Worth a single-file read of `external/src/index.ts` (or wherever the helper lives) before quoting any savings number in Public's own assistant configs.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:561:| Per-tool AI assistant profile generation (CLAUDE / Cursor / Codex / Copilot / Windsurf) | **002-codesight** | `generateProfileConfig()` is unique to this phase | adopt the "shared summary + per-tool overlay" split, write into namespaced sections only |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:580:3. **The MCP tool name `codesight_get_summary` referenced in the Claude profile.** The profile hardcodes 4 specific MCP tool calls (`codesight_get_summary`, `codesight_get_routes --prefix /api/users`, `codesight_get_blast_radius --file src/lib/db.ts`, `codesight_get_schema --model users`). These will only work if (a) the codesight MCP server is running and (b) the assistant has it configured. Public must NOT lift these literal tool names into its own profile generators without first ensuring an analogous MCP surface exists locally — or the generated CLAUDE.md will instruct the assistant to call tools that do not exist.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:598:| 4 | `iterations/iteration-004.md` | native fallback (codex stalled in S sleep ~40 min) | 6 | 0.55 | Q8 (per-tool profiles), Q10 (eval/fixtures) | 11.2x claim is README-only; ~14 frameworks have live route tests, only Drizzle+Prisma have schema tests |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:639:  - run 4: profile generators + benchmark validation (0.55)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:652:> User-requested continuation: "5 more iterations of /spec_kit:deep-research with gpt-5.4 high agents in fast mode through cli-codex." Started 2026-04-06T18:35:00Z. All 5 iterations dispatched in parallel via `codex exec --model gpt-5.4 -c model_reasoning_effort="high" --sandbox read-only -o /tmp/codex-iter-NNN-output.md`. The read-only sandbox blocked the agents' direct file writes to `/tmp` (`zsh:1: operation not permitted` and `can't create temp file for here document`), but in 4 of 5 cases the fully assembled report was preserved in the codex stdout reasoning trace before the failed write; the orchestrator (Claude Opus 4.6) extracted those reports verbatim into the iteration files. In the 5th case (iter 8) the captured `-o` last-message file already contained the agent's key findings with exact line citations, which the orchestrator reconstructed into a complete iteration file. No file modifications were made by codex agents themselves.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:661:- **Q16 (iter 9)** — Monorepo workspace aggregation, configuration surface, plugin/post-processor contract (`scanner.ts`, `config.ts`)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:691:**Iteration 9 — Monorepo, config, plugins** (6 findings, newInfoRatio 0.79)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:694:2. CLI flags are broad (`--init`, `--watch`, `--hook`, `--html`, `--mcp`, `--json`, `--benchmark`, `--profile`, `--blast`, `--telemetry`, `--eval`, plus output/depth/help/version), but **only `maxDepth`, `outputDir`, and `profile` flow into merged `CodesightConfig`**. The other flags stay as runtime booleans outside the persistent config object. (`external/src/index.ts:29-47, 300-363, 382-392`; `external/src/config.ts:98-107`)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:697:5. `disableDetectors` is a **true short-circuit** before invocation, not a post-filter. Disabled built-ins return resolved empty placeholders; plugin detectors still run. (`external/src/index.ts:99-130`)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:698:6. `CodesightPlugin` is a real typed contract (`name`, optional async `detector`, optional async `postProcessor`). Plugin detectors merge `routes`/`schemas`/`components`/`middleware` into the in-memory result; post-processors run after `scan()` returns and can replace the full `ScanResult`. **There are zero in-tree plugin examples or tests.** (`external/src/types.ts:141-170, 173-181`; `external/src/index.ts:115-133, 399-410`)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:716:| Per-tool profile overlay (CLAUDE/Cursor/Codex/Copilot/Windsurf) | iter 4 | medium | adopt now (with namespacing + no root-file clobbering) |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v2.md:743:- **Engine lineage:** 5 of 5 continuation iterations dispatched via `cli-codex --model gpt-5.4 -c model_reasoning_effort="high"`. Sandbox=read-only blocked agent file writes; orchestrator reconstructed all 5 iteration files from `-o` last-message capture (iter 8) or stdout reasoning trace (iters 6, 7, 9, 10).

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/research.md:26:The repository's strongest source-confirmed differentiators are (1) the unified `ScanResult` model in `external/src/index.ts:75-175` that branches late into static artifacts, MCP queries, profile files, and blast-radius analysis from one analysis pass; (2) the per-tool profile overlay system in `external/src/generators/ai-config.ts:164-264` that gives Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Copilot, and Windsurf meaningfully different instructions on top of one shared summary; (3) the genuine AST decorator-walking NestJS route detector in `external/src/ast/extract-routes.ts:157-248`; and (4) a small but real precision/recall/F1 fixture harness in `external/src/eval.ts:43-244`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/research.md:30:For `Code_Environment/Public`, the highest-leverage adoptions are the orchestration shape (one canonical scan, late-bound projections), the AST-first / regex-fallback / `confidence` label pattern, the per-tool profile overlay split, and the F1 fixture harness as a regression-test pattern. The biggest "do not duplicate" boundary is the cached MCP query interface, which belongs to phase 003-contextador, and the dependency-graph community detection layer, which belongs to phase 004-graphify.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:38:> Research the external repository at `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/external` and identify concrete improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around AST-based codebase analysis, framework/ORM detector architecture, AI-assistant context file generation, MCP tool design, and per-tool config profile patterns.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:44:- Per-tool AI assistant profile generation in `external/src/generators/ai-config.ts`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:65:**Iteration engine:** Per the user's request, every iteration was dispatched via `codex exec --model gpt-5.4 -c model_reasoning_effort="high" --sandbox read-only --ephemeral` with `-o /tmp/codex-iter-NNN-output.md` to capture the agent's last message. Iterations 1, 2, and 3 returned cleanly (~3 min each); iterations 4 and 5 each had codex stall in `S` sleep state for 20-50 minutes with no output, likely because of concurrent codex API traffic from a parallel session in another window. After killing the stuck processes the orchestrator (Claude Opus 4.6) completed iterations 4 and 5 with native `Read` / `Grep` tools, preserving exact line citations and the same finding template used by codex iterations.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:106:        +-- --profile <tool>      -> generateProfileConfig(result, root, tool)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:128:- `external/src/generators/ai-config.ts:1-264` — `generateAIConfigs` (5 files) + `generateProfileConfig` (5 profile cases)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:144:- **What it does:** `scan()` performs the core pipeline once: project detection → file collection → 7 parallel detectors → contract enrichment → `writeOutput` (twice, for placeholder and accurate token stats). `main()` then layers optional modes (JSON, HTML, benchmark, profile, blast-radius, MCP, eval, watch) on top of the same `result` object.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:153:- **What it does:** Both functions run `detectProject` + `collectFiles` + 7 parallel detectors + `enrichRouteContracts`. Differences: `scan()` respects `disableDetectors` config, runs plugins, prints CLI progress, calls `writeOutput` twice. `getScanResult()` uses fixed `maxDepth=10`, skips plugins, calls `writeOutput` once, caches the result. There is no shared helper.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:154:- **Why it matters:** Cautionary tale for `Code_Environment/Public`: factor any analogous pipeline into a single library function so toggles like disabled detectors and plugin hooks are not silently divergent across CLI and MCP surfaces.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:294:- **What it does:** Builds a compact ~1-2k token summary (stack, monorepo workspaces, route/model/env counts, top API areas, top 5 hot files, required env vars), then `switch (profile)`:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:301:- **Why it matters:** The single most directly transferable pattern in Codesight. Public could borrow the "shared summary + per-tool instruction overlay" split for `CLAUDE.md` / `.codex` / `.cursor` / `.gemini` directories. Cargo-cult risk: the Claude profile hardcodes `codesight_get_*` MCP tool names, which would only make sense after a similar MCP surface exists.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:304:- **Affected area:** AI-assistant context generation, per-tool config profiles
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:407:<!-- ANCHOR:per-tool-profile-diff -->
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:420:**Pattern to lift for `Code_Environment/Public`:** "Shared canonical summary + per-tool instruction overlay" with a `switch` over tool name and a small `summaryLines` array. Do NOT lift the hardcoded `codesight_get_*` MCP tool names from the Claude profile until an analogous MCP surface exists locally.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:422:<!-- /ANCHOR:per-tool-profile-diff -->
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:469:1. **Single-canonical-`ScanResult` orchestration shape.** Project one analysis result into multiple consumer surfaces (static markdown, MCP, profile files, blast-radius), branching late inside `main()` rather than running parallel pipelines. Anchor: `external/src/index.ts:75-175`. Public lift: define a shared `RepoFingerprint` (or similar) that any context-producing surface — CLAUDE.md generator, MCP tool, code-graph snapshot, spec-kit memory bootstrap — consumes from a single function. F-ORCH-1, F-ORCH-2.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:473:3. **Per-tool profile overlay split.** One shared canonical summary plus a `switch (profile)` of tool-specific instruction blocks. Anchor: `external/src/generators/ai-config.ts:164-264`. Public lift: build a `generateAssistantConfig(repoFingerprint, profile)` that emits CLAUDE.md / .codex / .gemini / .cursorrules with shared summary + tool-specific overlays. Do not hardcode MCP tool names that don't exist locally yet. F-PROFILE-2.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:493:2. **Per-tool profile generation in assistant directories.** The `generateAIConfigs` shared-summary writer is portable, but only `CLAUDE.md` has true upsert semantics in Codesight; the others would clobber existing files. Public must add marker comments / namespaced sections / `Code_Environment/Public/.claude/`-style directory namespacing before adopting. Anchor: `external/src/generators/ai-config.ts:71-158`. F-PROFILE-1.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:544:3. **Plugin API surface and contract.** Iteration 1 noted that `scan()` runs `userConfig.plugins` post-processors but the plugin contract was not explored. If Public adopts a plugin pattern, this is a small follow-up read of `external/src/index.ts:402-413` and the plugin type definitions in `external/src/types.ts`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:545:4. **Token-stat methodology.** `calculateTokenStats(...)` produces the `tokenStats.saved` value used in the Claude profile motivation message but was not traced. Worth a single-file read of `external/src/index.ts` (or wherever the helper lives) before quoting any savings number in Public's own assistant configs.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:561:| Per-tool AI assistant profile generation (CLAUDE / Cursor / Codex / Copilot / Windsurf) | **002-codesight** | `generateProfileConfig()` is unique to this phase | adopt the "shared summary + per-tool overlay" split, write into namespaced sections only |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:580:3. **The MCP tool name `codesight_get_summary` referenced in the Claude profile.** The profile hardcodes 4 specific MCP tool calls (`codesight_get_summary`, `codesight_get_routes --prefix /api/users`, `codesight_get_blast_radius --file src/lib/db.ts`, `codesight_get_schema --model users`). These will only work if (a) the codesight MCP server is running and (b) the assistant has it configured. Public must NOT lift these literal tool names into its own profile generators without first ensuring an analogous MCP surface exists locally — or the generated CLAUDE.md will instruct the assistant to call tools that do not exist.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:598:| 4 | `iterations/iteration-004.md` | native fallback (codex stalled in S sleep ~40 min) | 6 | 0.55 | Q8 (per-tool profiles), Q10 (eval/fixtures) | 11.2x claim is README-only; ~14 frameworks have live route tests, only Drizzle+Prisma have schema tests |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:639:  - run 4: profile generators + benchmark validation (0.55)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:652:> User-requested continuation: "5 more iterations of /spec_kit:deep-research with gpt-5.4 high agents in fast mode through cli-codex." Started 2026-04-06T18:35:00Z. All 5 iterations dispatched in parallel via `codex exec --model gpt-5.4 -c model_reasoning_effort="high" --sandbox read-only -o /tmp/codex-iter-NNN-output.md`. The read-only sandbox blocked the agents' direct file writes to `/tmp` (`zsh:1: operation not permitted` and `can't create temp file for here document`), but in 4 of 5 cases the fully assembled report was preserved in the codex stdout reasoning trace before the failed write; the orchestrator (Claude Opus 4.6) extracted those reports verbatim into the iteration files. In the 5th case (iter 8) the captured `-o` last-message file already contained the agent's key findings with exact line citations, which the orchestrator reconstructed into a complete iteration file. No file modifications were made by codex agents themselves.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:661:- **Q16 (iter 9)** — Monorepo workspace aggregation, configuration surface, plugin/post-processor contract (`scanner.ts`, `config.ts`)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:691:**Iteration 9 — Monorepo, config, plugins** (6 findings, newInfoRatio 0.79)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:694:2. CLI flags are broad (`--init`, `--watch`, `--hook`, `--html`, `--mcp`, `--json`, `--benchmark`, `--profile`, `--blast`, `--telemetry`, `--eval`, plus output/depth/help/version), but **only `maxDepth`, `outputDir`, and `profile` flow into merged `CodesightConfig`**. The other flags stay as runtime booleans outside the persistent config object. (`external/src/index.ts:29-47, 300-363, 382-392`; `external/src/config.ts:98-107`)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:697:5. `disableDetectors` is a **true short-circuit** before invocation, not a post-filter. Disabled built-ins return resolved empty placeholders; plugin detectors still run. (`external/src/index.ts:99-130`)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:698:6. `CodesightPlugin` is a real typed contract (`name`, optional async `detector`, optional async `postProcessor`). Plugin detectors merge `routes`/`schemas`/`components`/`middleware` into the in-memory result; post-processors run after `scan()` returns and can replace the full `ScanResult`. **There are zero in-tree plugin examples or tests.** (`external/src/types.ts:141-170, 173-181`; `external/src/index.ts:115-133, 399-410`)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:716:| Per-tool profile overlay (CLAUDE/Cursor/Codex/Copilot/Windsurf) | iter 4 | medium | adopt now (with namespacing + no root-file clobbering) |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/synthesis-v1.md:743:- **Engine lineage:** 5 of 5 continuation iterations dispatched via `cli-codex --model gpt-5.4 -c model_reasoning_effort="high"`. Sandbox=read-only blocked agent file writes; orchestrator reconstructed all 5 iteration files from `-o` last-message capture (iter 8) or stdout reasoning trace (iters 6, 7, 9, 10).

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/001-fix-command-dispatch/z_archive/049-system-analysis-bugs/research/research.md:284:- `profile.getDatabasePath()` creates per-provider database files

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/001-fix-command-dispatch/z_archive/049-system-analysis-bugs/research/research.md:378:1. Make `EMBEDDING_DIM` dynamic based on profile
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:6:      "id": "finding-1-27-affected-area-ai-assistant-context-generation-per-tool-config-profiles",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:7:      "text": "Affected area: AI-assistant context generation, per-tool config profiles",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:169:      "text": "What it does: `generateAIConfigs()` builds one generic narrative summary and writes or appends it into assistant-specific files such as `CLAUDE.md`, `.cursorrules`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, `codex.md`, and `AGENTS.md`. `generateProfileConfig()` then creates tighter tool-specific variants, with the Claude profile explicitly instructing the assistant to read `.codesight/CODESIGHT.md` first and use narrow MCP tools for routes, schema, and blast radius.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:181:      "text": "What it does: `scan()` performs the core pipeline once: `detectProject()`, `collectFiles()`, parallel detector fan-out, plugin detector merge, `enrichRouteContracts()`, `writeOutput()`, token-stat calculation, then a second `writeOutput()` with accurate token counts. `main()` loads config, runs that scan once, then layers optional modes on top of the same `result` object for telemetry, JSON, AI config generation, HTML, benchmark output, blast radius, profile generation, and watch mode.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:660:      "id": "finding-4-15-default-no-profile-falls-back-to-generateaiconfigs-and-writes-everything",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:661:      "text": "`default` (no profile) falls back to `generateAIConfigs()` and writes everything.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:678:      "id": "finding-4-19-affected-area-ai-assistant-context-generation-per-tool-config-profiles",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:679:      "text": "Affected area: AI-assistant context generation, per-tool config profiles",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:858:      "id": "finding-4-9-what-it-does-generateprofileconfig-result-root-profile-first-builds-a-compact-1-",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:859:      "text": "What it does: `generateProfileConfig(result, root, profile)` first builds a compact ~1-2k token summary (stack, monorepo workspaces, route/model/env counts, top API areas, top 5 hot files, required env vars), then `switch (profile)` writes meaningfully different content per target:",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:913:      "text": "Why it matters: This is the most directly transferable pattern in Codesight. `Code_Environment/Public` could borrow the \"shared summary + per-tool instruction overlay\" split for its `CLAUDE.md` / `.codex` / `.cursor` / `.gemini` directories, but the cargo-cult risk is real: Codesight's current implementation hardcodes example MCP tool names like `codesight_get_blast_radius` into the Claude profile, which would only make sense after a similar MCP surface is built locally.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:1056:      "id": "finding-5-29-source-external-src-index-ts-435-444-profile-generation-external-src-index-ts-49",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:1057:      "text": "Source: `external/src/index.ts:435-444` (profile generation), `external/src/index.ts:493-541` (blast-radius mode), `external/src/mcp-server.ts:89-130` (toolScan), `external/src/generators/ai-config.ts:71-158` (assistant file writers)",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:1093:      "text": "What it does: Both `scan()` and `getScanResult()` run `detectProject` + `collectFiles` + the 7 parallel detectors + `enrichRouteContracts`. The differences are: `scan()` respects `userConfig.disableDetectors`, runs plugin detectors and post-processors, prints CLI progress, calls `writeOutput` twice (placeholder then accurate token stats); `getScanResult()` skips the plugin path, uses fixed maxDepth=10, calls `writeOutput` once, caches the result. There is no shared helper.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:1105:      "text": "Why it matters: For `Code_Environment/Public`, the lesson is to factor any analogous \"scan + project\" pipeline into a single library function that both the CLI and any future MCP/server entry points call, so that toggles like disabled detectors, plugin hooks, and progress reporting are not silently divergent.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:1729:      "text": "Source: `external/src/generators/ai-config.ts:213` (raw `result.tokenStats.saved.toLocaleString()` in CLAUDE.md profile body), `external/src/formatter.ts:290` (`roundTo100(ts.saved).toLocaleString()` in `CODESIGHT.md` \"Token savings\" line), `external/src/index.ts:188-190` (CLI status uses raw `tokenStats.saved.toLocaleString()`), `external/src/index.ts:479-480` (CLI ASCII-art status block also uses raw)",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:1747:      "text": "What it does: `formatter.ts:290` writes \"this file is ~X tokens. Without it, AI exploration would cost ~Y tokens. **Saves ~Z tokens per conversation.**\" in `CODESIGHT.md`, with all three values passed through `roundTo100()` so the displayed numbers always end in `00` to signal estimation. But `ai-config.ts:213` writes \"This saves ~${result.tokenStats.saved.toLocaleString()} tokens per conversation.\" into the Claude profile file using the raw, unrounded number — which can show values like `12,847` and read like a real measurement.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:1795:      "text": "Why it matters for Code_Environment/Public: This is a small but meaningful UX inconsistency: the same upstream value is rounded for presentation in one surface and quoted unrounded in another, despite both being downstream consumers of the same heuristic formula. If we adopt the per-tool profile pattern (iter 4 finding 2), we should normalize the rounding rule across all surfaces — either always round-to-100 (signaling estimate) or always show raw (only honest if the formula is honest, which it is not). Pick rounding.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:1986:      "id": "finding-9-37-what-it-does-codesightconfig-exposes-plugins-codesightplugin-each-plugin-has-a-r",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:1987:      "text": "What it does: `CodesightConfig` exposes `plugins?: CodesightPlugin[]`; each plugin has a required `name`, optional async `detector(files, project)`, and optional async `postProcessor(result)`. Plugin detectors run after all built-ins, and can only merge additional `routes`, `schemas`, `components`, and `middleware` into the in-memory result set before contract enrichment/output. Post-processors run after `scan()` returns and can replace the final `ScanResult`. Within the files read, there are **no in-tree plugin implementations or plugin/disableDetectors tests** (`rg` over `external/tests` returned no matches), so third-party extensibility is inferred from the typed config-module surface rather than demonstrated by fixtures/tests.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:2011:      "text": "What it does: The CLI parser accepts `-o/--output`, `-d/--depth`, `--init`, `--watch`, `--hook`, `--html`, `--open`, `--mcp`, `--json`, `--benchmark`, `--profile <tool>`, `--blast <file>`, `--telemetry`, `--eval`, `-v/--version`, `-h/--help`, plus a positional directory argument. After parsing, `loadConfig(root)` is merged with CLI overrides only for `maxDepth`, `outputDir`, and `profile`; the rest stay as runtime booleans/strings outside the config object.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:2041:      "text": "Why it matters for Code_Environment/Public: Third-party detectors are possible if a user's config module imports or constructs plugin objects, but this is not a discovered package/plugin registry. Post-processors are a real extension point in code, not obviously vestigial, though they are currently unexercised in the visible test surface. For Public, the lesson is: plugin contracts that aren't tested aren't real. If we add plugin hooks, ship at least one in-tree fixture exercising them.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:2053:      "text": "Why it matters for Code_Environment/Public: This is a clean \"skip work, don't filter results\" pattern that's worth borrowing wholesale. It pairs naturally with the future possibility of replacing a built-in detector with a plugin (disable the built-in, enable the plugin), and avoids paying the runtime cost of detection just to throw it away.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:3324:      "id": "finding-17-6-affected-area-assistant-profile-generation",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:3325:      "text": "Affected area: assistant profile generation",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:3480:      "id": "finding-17-16-what-it-does-profile-mode-rewrites-cursorrules-codex-md-github-copilot-instructi",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:3481:      "text": "What it does: profile mode rewrites `.cursorrules`, `codex.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, and `.windsurfrules` directly. It also writes `AGENTS.md` at repo root when absent in default generation mode.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:3486:      "id": "finding-17-23-what-it-does-the-claude-profile-literally-instructs-the-assistant-to-call-codesi",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:3487:      "text": "What it does: the Claude profile literally instructs the assistant to call `codesight_get_summary`, `codesight_get_routes`, `codesight_get_blast_radius`, and `codesight_get_schema`.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:3492:      "id": "finding-17-30-what-it-does-the-claude-profile-says-this-saves-n-tokens-per-conversation-using-",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:3493:      "text": "What it does: the Claude profile says \"This saves ~N tokens per conversation\" using the unrounded `result.tokenStats.saved.toLocaleString()`.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:3510:      "id": "finding-17-24-why-it-matters-for-code-environment-public-public-should-adopt-the-profile-overl",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:3511:      "text": "Why it matters for Code_Environment/Public: Public should adopt the profile-overlay pattern, not these literal tool names. Hardcoding non-existent tools into a generated profile creates broken assistant guidance.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/002-codesight/research/findings-registry.json:4009:      "text": "What it does: watch mode implies more incrementalism than it has, MCP query tools write `.codesight/` on disk, profile generation mutates root assistant files, and token messaging regains false precision outside `CODESIGHT.md`.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/recommendations.md:99:**Rationale:** Combo 3 failed because provenance, evidence status, and freshness were treated as if one confidence field could carry all three. Public should first create an explicit contract for those axes and only then reconsider artifact-default structural packaging. The Claudest continuation supplies a concrete parallel in the analytics lane: transcript identity, replay checkpoints, and cache fields must be separated before consumer contracts become trustworthy. The Graphify closeout sharpened the structural half of the same lesson by showing that clustering, multimodal scope, and graph-first enrichment all fit better as later additive layers on current payloads than as a new graph-shaped authority surface. The CodeSight closeout reinforced the packaging risk directly: stale section files can persist, cold MCP queries write artifacts as a side effect, and profile/HTML projections can re-amplify heuristic token narratives, so shipping artifact-first surfaces before trust and freshness separation would harden the wrong contract. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:27:The right lesson is not to drop the gates. It is to classify them. New work and ambiguous scope should stay fully conversational. Bound continuation work, packet-local leaf work, and read-only review work should have gate profiles that mark certain gates as pre-satisfied or auto-derived from mode. Runtime sentinels and capability checks can then carry more of the safety burden.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:32:finding: `system-spec-kit` should pivot from one universal conversational gate sequence to a small set of operating profiles, including a packet-bound continuation profile where scope, routing, and some save behavior are pre-resolved.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:38:- **Prerequisites:** define the allowed operating profiles and which gates become implicit in each
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:48:- **If REFACTOR/PIVOT/SIMPLIFY — concrete proposal:** Define profiles such as `new-work`, `bound-continuation`, and `read-only-review`; let the profile determine which gates are explicit, implicit, or enforced by runtime sentinels.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:53:I looked for existing local guidance that already formalizes profile-based gate shortcuts. I found session persistence for Gate 3, but not a broader profile model for continuation work.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:57:- Should runtime profiles live in command YAML, constitutional policy, or a dedicated machine-readable manifest?
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:58:- Does the orchestrator architecture need to change for profile-based gateing to be maintainable?

The local lifecycle split is internally coherent, but it leaks too much workflow machinery into the operator experience. A single primary command with explicit profiles would better match the external repo's lower-friction entry surface.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:27:The best import from the external repo is not "use exactly one command for everything." It is "make the operator choose the outcome, not the internal engine." `system-spec-kit` can keep separate internal workflows, but the public surface should be one primary lifecycle command with profiles like `new-work`, `continue-bound-work`, and `full-lifecycle`, rather than three sibling commands that each restate the machine room.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:32:finding: `system-spec-kit` should merge the public `plan` / `implement` / `complete` lifecycle into one profile-driven entry surface, while keeping the current internal workflow separation behind that surface.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/001-fix-command-dispatch/z_archive/027-memory-plugin-and-refinement/001-memory-repo-analysis/scratch/legacy-memory-quarantine/17-12-25_14-00__roampal-analysis.md:116:- **Technical Implementation Details** - pluginPath: .
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/001-fix-command-dispatch/z_archive/027-memory-plugin-and-refinement/001-memory-repo-analysis/scratch/legacy-memory-quarantine/17-12-25_14-00__roampal-analysis.md:149:Implemented the Memory Context Plugin for OpenCode (.opencode/plugin/memory-context.js). Used Sequential Thinking MCP for deep analysis of OpenCode plugin architecture. Discovered key hooks: experimental.chat.system.transform (for system prompt injection), chat.message (for trigger logging), and event handler (for session lifecycle). Plugin implements Layer 1 (constitutional context injection via system transform), Layer 2 (trigger matching with logging), and Layer 3 placeholder (exchange recording). Uses Bun's native bun:sqlite for zero dependencies. Plugin is 302 lines with caching, graceful degradation, and token budgeting (~500 tokens max for constitutional context).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/001-fix-command-dispatch/z_archive/027-memory-plugin-and-refinement/001-memory-repo-analysis/scratch/legacy-memory-quarantine/17-12-25_14-00__roampal-analysis.md:152:- Implemented the Memory Context Plugin for OpenCode (.opencode/plugin/memory-context.js). .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:12:I compared the local `orchestrate.md` profile with the external orchestrator agent, orchestrator command, and orchestrator guard. I focused on the number of responsibilities each surface carries and how much of the enforcement model depends on one very large prompt.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/001-fix-command-dispatch/z_archive/083-speckit-reimagined-test-suite/decision-record.md:846:- ESLint plugin for snapshot rules (optional)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/iteration-1.md:35:- Phase 005 open questions note that `external/plugins/claude-memory/CLAUDE.md` does not exist in this checkout.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:25:Phase 2 makes the mismatch clearer than Phase 1 did: `system-spec-kit` effectively has two documentation models already. One is the general feature packet with levels, checklists, and decision records. The other is the deep-loop packet with workflow-owned state and synthesis files. The current system treats the second model as a specialized overlay rather than a first-class profile. That creates avoidable complexity because research-only work ends up mentally translated through the broader feature-document ladder even when the actual runtime contract lives almost entirely under `research/`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:27:The external repo's answer is not better because it is sparse; it is better because it is honest about the job being done. A loop-driven research run needs a charter, state, evidence, synthesis, and a stop condition. It does not inherently need the full general feature packet semantics. `system-spec-kit` should keep the richer documentation path for implementation packets, but formalize a lighter "research packet" profile rather than leaving it half-inside and half-outside the main level taxonomy.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:32:finding: `system-spec-kit` should formalize a minimal research-packet profile for deep-research and deep-review phases. The current system already behaves that way in practice, but the core documentation model does not clearly acknowledge it.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:43:- **Current system-spec-kit approach:** General work is framed through Level 1/2/3+ packet depth, then optionally overlaid with phases; deep research has a separate runtime packet shape, but that shape is not clearly elevated as its own documentation profile.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:48:- **If REFACTOR/PIVOT/SIMPLIFY — concrete proposal:** Add a first-class "research packet minimal profile" that canonically requires `phase-research-prompt.md` plus `research/` state/synthesis artifacts, without pretending those runs map to the full Level 1 baseline.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:50:- **Migration path:** Document the profile first, then update validators and templates so existing research packets remain valid without forcing retroactive packet reshaping.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:53:I looked for a clear local statement that research packets are already exempt from the broader level system. I found a dedicated research-state model, but not a crisp first-class profile that resolves the overlap cleanly.

[SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/pyproject.toml:1-10] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/README.md:15-29] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/README.md:11-22]

[SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/pyproject.toml:1-10] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/README.md:11-22] [SOURCE: /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/SKILL.md:415-421] |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/q-e-license-runtime.md:46:Claudest is similarly split. The root manifest requires only Python `>=3.7`, and the `claude-memory` plugin README makes clear that the hook/search stack is stdlib-only Python plus SQLite. That lines up well with Public's partial Python support and existing SQLite-heavy memory layer. [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/pyproject.toml:1-10] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/README.md:9-14]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/q-e-license-runtime.md:48:But two gates keep it out of `source-portable`. First, the checkout has no `005-claudest/external/LICENSE` file; second, the runtime assumes Claude's plugin marketplace and Claude lifecycle hooks rather than Public-native entrypoints. So the system is best scored `mixed`: many implementation ideas are technically reusable, but direct code adoption remains blocked today. [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/README.md:15-29] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/README.md:72-80] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/README.md:199-201]

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/001-fix-command-dispatch/z_archive/027-memory-plugin-and-refinement/003-memory-plugin-debugging/spec.md:22:The semantic memory plugin fails to load memories into the AI context dashboard, showing "Loaded 0 memories for dashboard" despite 66+ memories existing in the database. This prevents the AI from having automatic access to relevant context at session start.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/001-fix-command-dispatch/z_archive/027-memory-plugin-and-refinement/003-memory-plugin-debugging/spec.md:26:The plugin uses `better-sqlite3`, a native Node.js addon that is incompatible with Bun runtime. OpenCode runs plugins in-process using Bun, not as separate Node.js processes like MCP servers.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/combo-stress-test-iter-12.md:16:1. **Lossy warm-start summary**: the deterministic summary intentionally disables markers and, for long sessions, keeps only the first 2 and last 6 exchanges. If the missing middle contains the decisive architectural fork, the combo starts "warm" but wrong. [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/skills/recall-conversations/scripts/memory_lib/summarizer.py:251-274] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/skills/recall-conversations/scripts/memory_lib/summarizer.py:343-349]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/combo-stress-test-iter-12.md:17:2. **Wrong prior session chosen**: startup selection only inspects the 20 most recent root sessions, skips 1-exchange sessions, stops at the first substantive session, and lets `/clear` handoff expire after 30 seconds. Relevant context can be missed even when it exists. [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/hooks/memory-context.py:51-62] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/hooks/memory-context.py:150-160] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/hooks/memory-context.py:218-250]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/combo-stress-test-iter-12.md:18:3. **Producer drift silently degrades the fast path**: the cached summary depends on Stop/import producers that parse undocumented Claude JSONL and only persist what that adapter can recover. Phase 001 already flags that parser shape as a guarded adapter, not stable infra. [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/hooks/sync_current.py:101-189] [SOURCE: 001-claude-optimization-settings/research/research.md:192-196] [SOURCE: 001-claude-optimization-settings/research/research.md:335-339]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/combo-stress-test-iter-12.md:23:- **Reliable producer completion**: Stop/import hooks must run successfully enough that SessionStart usually hits a cached `context_summary`, not the slower fallback path. [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/hooks/memory-context.py:88-113] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/hooks/sync_current.py:78-99]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/combo-stress-test-iter-12.md:24:- **Summary fidelity to first-follow-up needs**: the missing middle of long sessions must not contain the exact state needed for the next structural query. [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/skills/recall-conversations/scripts/memory_lib/summarizer.py:256-274]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/combo-stress-test-iter-12.md:29:- Component A vs Component B: Stop-time summary + SessionStart fast path do compose, but only as a continuity layer; they do not prove that the right retrieval owner is structural rather than memory-first. [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/hooks/memory-context.py:173-250]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/combo-stress-test-iter-12.md:30:- Component A vs Component C: the summary is project-local conversational recall, while the hook assumes the first corrective move is graph-oriented. That is a task-shape collision, not a schema collision. [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/hooks/memory-context.py:173-250] [SOURCE: 004-graphify/external/graphify/__main__.py:9-21]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/combo-stress-test-iter-12.md:38:- [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/skills/recall-conversations/scripts/memory_lib/summarizer.py:251-274] Claudest's own summary producer is a direct counter-example to the "warm and right" assumption. For long sessions it removes markers entirely and reduces the transcript to first-2 plus last-6 exchanges. That is a valid compression tactic, but it means the first live follow-up can be routed from a summary that has already discarded the exact middle-of-session branch choice that should have determined whether the next action is `code_graph_query`, semantic search, or manual recovery.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/combo-stress-test-iter-12.md:53:2. **Unknown models get false pricing precision**: Claudest's pricing helper silently defaults unknown models to Sonnet rates. That keeps the dashboard populated, but it also means mixed-model fleets can look more accurate than they are. [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/skills/get-token-insights/scripts/ingest_token_data.py:72-80]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/combo-stress-test-iter-12.md:54:3. **Some token classes are still heuristic**: unclassified cache creation is billed as 5-minute cache write, and thinking tokens are approximated from text length rather than counted by the provider. The spend model is normalized, not authoritative. [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/skills/get-token-insights/scripts/ingest_token_data.py:82-96] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/skills/get-token-insights/scripts/ingest_token_data.py:492-496]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/combo-stress-test-iter-12.md:56:5. **Raw-session portability is conditional, not guaranteed**: phase 001 is explicit that Claude JSONL parsing is an undocumented adapter and that cross-agent portability only exists when each runtime emits usable raw session artifacts. The combo can become Claude-shaped observability masquerading as general measurement. [SOURCE: 001-claude-optimization-settings/research/research.md:192-196] [SOURCE: 001-claude-optimization-settings/research/research.md:335-339] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/skills/get-token-insights/scripts/ingest_token_data.py:394-580]

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:25:The external council pattern's strongest idea is not "always use three models." It is that disagreement can be treated as a structured input to synthesis instead of a nuisance. That could make `system-spec-kit`'s research output stronger on ambiguous topics, especially architecture or standards research. The downside is operational complexity: extra providers, extra cost, and extra failure modes. Because `system-spec-kit` already supports multiple runtimes and model families indirectly, the right transfer is an optional council mode or synthesis profile, not a replacement for the default loop.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/library/specializations/game-development/skills/unreal-development/SKILL.md:3:description: Unreal Engine integration skill for C++/Blueprint development, actor lifecycle management, plugin development, and editor automation. Enables LLMs to interact with Unreal Editor through MCP servers for level manipulation, Blueprint generation, and automated workflows.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/library/specializations/game-development/skills/unreal-development/SKILL.md:20:- Configure plugin dependencies
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/library/specializations/game-development/skills/unreal-development/SKILL.md:402:- [ClaudeAI Plugin for UE5](https://claudeaiplugin.com/)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:28:So the right move is not to delete spec folders. The right move is to redesign the surface language. Keep the current file contracts and validators internally, but present them as named work profiles with progressive reveal. Let operators choose "Quick change" or "Architecture packet" and let the system map that to the current template stack.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:33:finding: `system-spec-kit` should redesign spec-folder UX around named work profiles instead of asking operators to reason directly about Level 1/2/3+ template classes.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:39:- **Prerequisites:** define stable profile names and a reversible mapping to current levels
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:48:- **What system-spec-kit should ADD for better UX:** Add named work profiles that map to current file sets and reveal extra artifact requirements only when needed.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:52:I looked for evidence that numeric documentation levels are a clearer operator metaphor than named work profiles. I did not find support for that framing in the external repo.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/005-intellegix-code-agent-toolkit-master/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:56:- Should research packets and implementation packets become separate named profile families?
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/citation-audit-iter-11.json:146:      "title": "Per-tool profile overlay split",

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/iteration-3.md:104:**Evidence:** [SOURCE: phase-5/research/research.md:107-113] [SOURCE: phase-5/implementation-summary.md:73-73] [SOURCE: phase-5/implementation-summary.md:125-125] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/skills/recall-conversations/scripts/memory_lib/db.py:115-188] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/skills/recall-conversations/scripts/memory_lib/db.py:195-205]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/iteration-3.md:105:**Answer:** The Claudest pattern is source-real: it has alternate FTS5 and FTS4 schemas plus a PRAGMA-driven capability probe. That validates phase 5's narrowed Public recommendation: trust the smallest safe v1 only as `fts5_bm25 -> like_scan` until a packet actually adds alternate FTS4 DDL on the Public side. [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/skills/recall-conversations/scripts/memory_lib/db.py:115-188] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/skills/recall-conversations/scripts/memory_lib/db.py:195-205] [SOURCE: phase-5/implementation-summary.md:125-125]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/iteration-3.md:115:### G5.CMD — The claude-memory plugin-specific CLAUDE.md is absent in this checkout
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/iteration-3.md:119:**Answer:** The anomaly holds. This checkout has a root `external/CLAUDE.md` for the whole marketplace repo, but no `external/plugins/claude-memory/CLAUDE.md`, so any plugin-local instruction layer still has to be reconstructed indirectly. [SOURCE: phase-5/research/research.md:444-444] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/CLAUDE.md:1-5]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/iteration-3.md:125:**Evidence:** [SOURCE: phase-5/research/research.md:198-200] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/hooks/memory-context.py:253-257] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/hooks/memory-context.py:311-346] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/skills/recall-conversations/scripts/memory_lib/summarizer.py:343-349]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/iterations/iteration-3.md:126:**Answer:** The docstring drift is fully source-proven. `_build_fallback_context()` still claims a last-3-exchanges render, but the implementation follows the summarizer's actual contract: render all exchanges for short sessions, otherwise render first 2, gap, and last 6. [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/hooks/memory-context.py:253-257] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/hooks/memory-context.py:311-346] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/external/plugins/claude-memory/skills/recall-conversations/scripts/memory_lib/summarizer.py:343-349]

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/023-hybrid-rag-fusion-refinement/013-fts5-fix-and-search-dashboard/spec.md:60:The root cause chain: `shared/embeddings.ts` initializes the Voyage-4 provider lazily (lines 364–417); `shared/embeddings/profile.ts::getDatabasePath()` (lines 63–71) returns a provider-derived name; `vector-index-store.ts::resolve_database_path()` reads that derived name each call; `db-state.ts::reinitializeDatabase()` (lines 245–293) rebinds to the newly resolved path, abandoning the populated DB.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/023-hybrid-rag-fusion-refinement/013-fts5-fix-and-search-dashboard/spec.md:112:| `shared/embeddings/profile.ts` | Modify | Harden `getDatabasePath()` (lines 63–71) to not silently fall back to empty provider DB |

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/001-fix-command-dispatch/z_archive/027-memory-plugin-and-refinement/003-memory-plugin-debugging/research/research.md:3:description: "Problem: The memory plugin shows \"Loaded 0 memories for dashboard\" despite 66 memories existing in the database."

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/001-fix-command-dispatch/z_archive/027-memory-plugin-and-refinement/003-memory-plugin-debugging/research/research.md:20:**Problem**: The memory plugin shows "Loaded 0 memories for dashboard" despite 66 memories existing in the database.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/001-fix-command-dispatch/z_archive/027-memory-plugin-and-refinement/003-memory-plugin-debugging/research/research.md:22:**Root Cause**: The plugin uses `better-sqlite3`, a native Node.js addon that is incompatible with Bun runtime. OpenCode runs plugins in-process using Bun, not Node.js.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/001-fix-command-dispatch/z_archive/027-memory-plugin-and-refinement/003-memory-plugin-debugging/research/research.md:31:**Solution**: Migrate the plugin from `better-sqlite3` to `bun:sqlite` (Bun's native SQLite implementation).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/001-fix-command-dispatch/z_archive/027-memory-plugin-and-refinement/003-memory-plugin-debugging/research/research.md:56:The MCP server runs as a **separate Node.js process** spawned by OpenCode, communicating via stdio. The plugin runs **in-process** with OpenCode, which uses Bun as its runtime.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/001-fix-command-dispatch/z_archive/027-memory-plugin-and-refinement/003-memory-plugin-debugging/research/research.md:63:- The plugin API exposes `$` as Bun's shell API (typed as `BunShell`)

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/research.md:31:- The 2026-04-08 CodeSight closeout tightened the phase-002 lane without changing the master ordering. What survives more strongly now is projection discipline: one canonical scan feeding conditional markdown artifacts, per-tool profile overlays, honest AST/regex provenance, and frozen detector fixtures. What weakened is any temptation to copy the whole surface: watch and hook automation are repo-mutating workflow choices, middleware/libs/config layers are breadcrumb-grade, the MCP surface is cached and text-first with cold-query side effects, and HTML remains a projection convenience rather than a product priority. [SOURCE: 002-codesight/research/research.md:24-30] [SOURCE: 002-codesight/research/research.md:309-325] [SOURCE: 002-codesight/research/research.md:772-860]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/research.md:45:CodeSight still stands out as the cleanest Node-native packaging reference in the set, and the 20-iteration closeout made that conclusion more precise. The strongest transferable ideas are now clearly projection-oriented: one canonical scan output, conditional artifact emission, per-tool profile overlays, honest AST/regex provenance, and a frozen F1 fixture harness. The same closeout also drew firmer boundaries around what should NOT be copied wholesale: watch mode is only a UX shell over full rescans, `installGitHook()` is repo-mutating automation, middleware/libs/config detectors are low-authority breadcrumbs, the MCP surface is cached text with cold-query write side effects, HTML is only a late-bound projection, and root assistant-file generation remains unsafe without namespacing. Public should borrow CodeSight’s packaging, detector harnessing, and provenance discipline, while explicitly not copying the side-effectful query model, root-file mutation, or heuristic token narrative. [SOURCE: 002-codesight/research/research.md:24-30] [SOURCE: 002-codesight/research/research.md:285-325] [SOURCE: 002-codesight/research/research.md:772-860]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/research.md:54:Claudest remains the most composition-friendly pattern source for Public, but v2 now has a much sharper reason for saying so. It still does not mean “best portable source code”: the license story remains incomplete in this checkout, the host assumptions remain Claude-plugin-shaped, and the producer side still omits some analytics-grade fields. What it does mean is that Claudest’s strongest ideas already land inside one owner Public has, and the 20-iteration continuation converted those ideas into a packet-ordered implementation lane instead of leaving them as generic “adopt now” advice. The packet now points to one concrete first move inside Public’s continuity/analytics track: a shared FTS capability helper plus forced-degrade tests, followed by a bounded Stop-hook metadata patch, a reader-owned analytics model, an additive cached-summary fast path, an explicit verifier/discoverer workflow split, and finally portable token-observability contracts. Cached summaries can still be lossy and wrong, startup selection can still be heuristic, and dashboard-grade spend views can still overstate certainty when defaults or estimated token classes are not visibly labeled. That mix keeps Claudest as the primary reference for Public’s next implementation wave, but only if Public imports the humility, boundary discipline, and acceptance-gate logic alongside the feature shape. [SOURCE: 005-claudest/research/research.md:17-19] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/research/research.md:625-668] [SOURCE: research/iterations/combo-stress-test-iter-12.md:15-44] [SOURCE: research/iterations/combo-stress-test-iter-12.md:51-80]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/research.md:279:The third pattern is a design-direction warning more than a registry headline, but it is one of the most important v2 additions. CodeSight’s generated profiles tell the agent to read the static artifact first and query live surfaces second, without a strong freshness contract. Graphify’s hook only helps if the graph already exists and is current. Claudest’s summary fast path is explicitly lossy and can select the wrong prior session. Across three systems, durable context is introduced early because it feels efficient and ergonomic, yet the freshness and authority boundary is left loose enough that the durable surface can become a wrong first source of truth. [SOURCE: research/iterations/cross-phase-patterns-iter-15.md:57-75]
