.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/001-fix-command-dispatch/z_archive/027-memory-plugin-and-refinement/002-memory-plugin/scratch/legacy-memory-quarantine/16-02-26_14-50__mcp-server-fixes.md:392:<!-- /ANCHOR:metadata-session-legacy-1770632216916-nru5fa-003-memory-and-spec-kit/z-archive/027-memory-plugin-and-refinement/002-memory-plugin -->
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/archive/research-v2-pre-codesight-closeout.md:41:CodeSight still stands out as the cleanest Node-native packaging reference in the set. It has one canonical scan output, assistant-profile overlays, artifact generation, and a detector culture strong enough to justify importing the idea of frozen fixtures before widening structural extraction. v2, however, is blunter about its honesty problems. The 11.2x token claim remains formula-driven rather than provider-counted, the Go path still badges regex-heavy extraction as `ast`, and the blast-radius lane still lacks any scored evaluation despite README language that suggests otherwise. Iter-10 did close one ambiguity in CodeSight’s favor: monorepo support is not vague; it is concretely bounded to `pnpm-workspace.yaml` and `package.json workspaces`, which means npm/yarn workspace repos are in scope, while nx/turbo/lerna/rush-only shapes are not. Public should borrow CodeSight’s packaging, detector harnessing, and AST/regex provenance discipline, but not its benchmark language, not its scan-first orchestration shape, and not its trust labels verbatim. [SOURCE: 002-codesight/research/research.md:243-255] [SOURCE: 002-codesight/research/research.md:338-341] [SOURCE: 002-codesight/research/research.md:668-681] [SOURCE: research/iterations/gap-reattempt-iter-10.json:118-171] [SOURCE: research/iterations/gap-reattempt-iter-10.json:172-259] [SOURCE: research/iterations/cross-phase-patterns-iter-15.md:15-30]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/archive/research-v2-pre-codesight-closeout.md:50:Claudest remains the most composition-friendly pattern source for Public, but v2 now has a much sharper reason for saying so. It still does not mean “best portable source code”: the license story remains incomplete in this checkout, the host assumptions remain Claude-plugin-shaped, and the producer side still omits some analytics-grade fields. What it does mean is that Claudest’s strongest ideas already land inside one owner Public has, and the 20-iteration continuation converted those ideas into a packet-ordered implementation lane instead of leaving them as generic “adopt now” advice. The packet now points to one concrete first move inside Public’s continuity/analytics track: a shared FTS capability helper plus forced-degrade tests, followed by a bounded Stop-hook metadata patch, a reader-owned analytics model, an additive cached-summary fast path, an explicit verifier/discoverer workflow split, and finally portable token-observability contracts. Cached summaries can still be lossy and wrong, startup selection can still be heuristic, and dashboard-grade spend views can still overstate certainty when defaults or estimated token classes are not visibly labeled. That mix keeps Claudest as the primary reference for Public’s next implementation wave, but only if Public imports the humility, boundary discipline, and acceptance-gate logic alongside the feature shape. [SOURCE: 005-claudest/research/research.md:17-19] [SOURCE: 005-claudest/research/research.md:625-668] [SOURCE: research/iterations/combo-stress-test-iter-12.md:15-44] [SOURCE: research/iterations/combo-stress-test-iter-12.md:51-80]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/archive/research-v2-pre-codesight-closeout.md:273:The third pattern is a design-direction warning more than a registry headline, but it is one of the most important v2 additions. CodeSight’s generated profiles tell the agent to read the static artifact first and query live surfaces second, without a strong freshness contract. Graphify’s hook only helps if the graph already exists and is current. Claudest’s summary fast path is explicitly lossy and can select the wrong prior session. Across three systems, durable context is introduced early because it feels efficient and ergonomic, yet the freshness and authority boundary is left loose enough that the durable surface can become a wrong first source of truth. [SOURCE: research/iterations/cross-phase-patterns-iter-15.md:57-75]

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/archive/research-v1-iter-8.md:12:- Treat CodeSight as a packaging and detector-discipline reference, not as a verified token-savings benchmark: its strongest transferable pieces are profile overlays, fixture-based detector regression, AST/regex provenance honesty, and honest naming of centrality heuristics. [SOURCE: research/iterations/q-a-token-honesty.md:42-53] [SOURCE: research/iterations/q-c-composition-risk.md:22-28]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/archive/research-v1-iter-8.md:30:Claudest finished as the most practically reusable system even though it was not the cleanest legal/runtime fit. Its key strength is continuity discipline: cached `context_summary` startup, deterministic Stop-time production, FTS capability detection, consolidation contracts, pricing/cache normalization, and explicit auditor-vs-discoverer roles all reinforce the owner that Public already calls Spec Kit Memory. Its biggest weakness is portability evidence rather than design quality: the snapshot lacks a canonical `LICENSE` file, the host model assumes Claude plugin lifecycles, and Public still lacks the producer-side fields needed to reproduce Claudest-grade analytics honestly. Public should still treat Claudest as the primary reference for the next implementation wave, because its best ideas compose inside one existing surface instead of pressuring the architecture toward a monolith. [SOURCE: phase-5/research/research.md:107-113] [SOURCE: phase-5/research/research.md:171-220] [SOURCE: phase-5/research/research.md:273-320] [SOURCE: phase-5/research/research.md:395-405] [SOURCE: research/iterations/iteration-3.md:101-134]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/archive/research-v1-iter-8.md:85:The safest fast wins cluster tightly. Claudest’s cached `context_summary` fast path, deterministic Stop-time summaries, cache-cliff metrics, dashboard contracts, and browse/search separation all fit within Spec Kit Memory. Graphify’s PreToolUse hook and validator help the model or the pipeline respect existing boundaries rather than replacing them. CodeSight’s F1 harness and profile overlays stay as packaging or regression tooling beside the three owner surfaces. In other words, the winning moves add leverage at the seam without moving ownership. [SOURCE: research/iterations/q-c-composition-risk.md:11-16] [SOURCE: research/iterations/q-c-composition-risk.md:77-131]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/archive/research-v1-iter-8.md:178:| 3 | Per-tool profile overlay split | 002 | mixed | new surface | low | Static assistant-profile generation sits beside the three core surfaces. |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/archive/research-v1-iter-8.md:206:- `3` Per-tool profile overlay split (`phase 002`, risk `low`, effort `M`)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/archive/research-v1-iter-8.md:272:| 005 Claudest | `unclear` | `Python >=3.7` Claude plugin/hooks stack | `partial` | `mixed` | Portable ideas exist, but the repo snapshot lacks a canonical `LICENSE` file and assumes Claude plugin hosts. |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/archive/research-v1-iter-8.md:304:- Effort: M. Prerequisites: AST/regex honesty, profile/overlay packaging, validator-backed evidence tags. [SOURCE: research/iterations/q-f-killer-combos.md:63-69] [SOURCE: research/iterations/q-d-adoption-sequencing.md:21-26] [SOURCE: research/iterations/q-d-adoption-sequencing.md:35-40]

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/CLAUDE.md:472:Consolidates CSS classes after build for 50% faster style recalculation with zero runtime overhead. Auto-included as a devDependency in CSS-enabled profiles; runs via `pnpm build:optimize` (also auto-runs as `postbuild`).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/CLAUDE.md:482:Gives AI agents direct database access through 14 MCP tools with full guardrails, sanitization, and error handling. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:98:- **27 Slash Commands** (17 project + 10 kit management) — `/mdd`, `/help`, `/quickstart`, `/install-global`, `/setup`, `/show-user-guide`, `/diagram`, `/review`, `/commit`, `/progress`, `/test-plan`, `/architecture`, `/new-project`, `/security-check`, `/optimize-docker`, `/create-e2e`, `/create-api`, `/worktree`, `/what-is-my-ai-doing`, `/refactor`, `/set-project-profile-default`, `/add-project-setup`, `/projects-created`, `/remove-project`, `/convert-project-to-starter-kit`, `/update-project`, `/add-feature`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:158:│   ├── 02-profile-system.md
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:197:> **Full disclosure:** These packages are developed by the same person who maintains this starter kit. They are completely open source (MIT license), and the starter kit works fully without them. ClassMCP and Classpresso are auto-included in CSS-enabled profiles because they directly complement the AI-assisted CSS workflow this kit teaches. StrictDB-MCP is auto-included in database-enabled profiles. TerseJSON is documented but not auto-included.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:201:MCP server that provides semantic CSS class patterns to Claude, reducing token usage when generating or editing styles. Auto-added to the `mcp` field in CSS-enabled profiles.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:211:Gives AI agents direct database access through 14 MCP tools with full guardrails, sanitization, and error handling. Auto-included in database-enabled profiles (`mcp` field in `claude-mastery-project.conf`).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:221:Consolidates CSS classes after build for 50% faster style recalculation with zero runtime overhead. Auto-added as a devDependency in CSS-enabled profiles. Runs automatically after `pnpm build`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:241:This starter kit works with any language, framework, or database. Use `/new-project my-app clean` for zero opinions, or pick a profile that matches your stack.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:255:| **CSS** | Tailwind CSS + ClassMCP + Classpresso | ClassMCP (MCP) + Classpresso (post-build) auto-included in CSS profiles |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:413:│   │   ├── set-project-profile-default.md # /set-project-profile-default — set default profile
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:414:│   │   ├── add-project-setup.md  # /add-project-setup — create a named profile
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:470:├── claude-mastery-project.conf  # /new-project profiles + global root_dir
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:788:Interactive first-run walkthrough for new users. Checks if global config is installed, asks for a project name and profile preference, then walks you through the first 5 minutes: scaffolding, setup, first dev server, first review, first commit. Designed for someone who just cloned the starter kit and doesn't know where to start.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:896:### `/set-project-profile-default`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:898:Sets the default profile for `/new-project`. Accepts any profile name: `clean`, `default`, `go`, `vue`, `python-api`, etc. Also supports shorthand to create a custom default: `/set-project-profile-default mongo next tailwind docker` creates a `[user-default]` profile with those settings.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:902:Interactive wizard that walks you through creating a named profile in `claude-mastery-project.conf`. Asks about language, framework, database, hosting, package manager, analytics, options, and MCP servers. The new profile can then be used with `/new-project my-app <profile-name>`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:906:Lists every project scaffolded by `/new-project`, with creation date, profile used, language, framework, database, and location. Checks which projects still exist on disk and marks missing ones. Data is stored in `~/.claude/starter-kit-projects.json` (global — shared across all starter kit instances).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:979:Full project scaffolding with profiles:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:1001:**`default`** and other profiles — Full opinionated scaffolding with project type, framework, SSR, hosting (Dokploy/Vercel/static), package manager, database, extras (Tailwind, Prisma, Docker, CI), and MCP servers. Use `claude-mastery-project.conf` to save your preferred stack.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:1610:**How it works:** Uses Playwright's accessibility tree (not screenshots) — fast, lightweight, and LLM-friendly. Supports Chrome, Firefox, WebKit, and 143+ device emulation profiles
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:1624:MCP server that provides semantic CSS class patterns to Claude, reducing token usage when working with styles. Instead of Claude guessing class names or hallucinating utility classes, ClassMCP feeds it the correct patterns from your project's CSS framework. Auto-included in all CSS-enabled profiles.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/README.md:1641:Gives AI agents direct database access through 14 MCP tools with full guardrails, sanitization, and error handling. Instead of Claude generating raw queries or connection code, StrictDB-MCP provides structured tools for reading, writing, and managing data across all supported backends. Auto-included in database-enabled profiles.

The `clean` profile gives you all AI tooling with zero coding opinions.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/USER_GUIDE.md:454:#### `/set-project-profile-default`

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/USER_GUIDE.md:466:Interactive wizard to create a named profile in `claude-mastery-project.conf`. Asks about language, framework, database, hosting, package manager, analytics, and MCP servers.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/USER_GUIDE.md:470:Lists every project scaffolded by `/new-project` — creation date, profile, language, framework, database, and location. Checks which still exist on disk.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/USER_GUIDE.md:1171:A: Yes. Use `/new-project my-app clean` to get zero coding opinions — including no TypeScript enforcement. The `default` profile enforces TypeScript because it's a best practice for AI-assisted development, but `clean` mode lets you choose.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/USER_GUIDE.md:1177:A: Yes. The `default` profile uses pnpm, but you can specify any package manager during `/new-project` setup or change it in `claude-mastery-project.conf`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/USER_GUIDE.md:1196:A: StrictDB is recommended for AI-assisted development because it provides a simpler mental model with fewer abstractions. But for `clean` profile projects, you can use any ORM or ODM you prefer.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/USER_GUIDE.md:1221:A: The `check-rybbit.sh` hook only activates if `analytics = rybbit` is set in your project profile. If you don't use Rybbit, set `analytics = none` or use the `clean` profile. The check prevents deploying without analytics tracking.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/003-claude-code-mastery-project-starter-kit-main/external/USER_GUIDE.md:1224:A: Yes. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/a5c/marketplace/plugins/agentsh/configure.md:3:This plugin supports several configuration options. All configuration is stored in `.a5c/agentsh/`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/a5c/marketplace/plugins/agentsh/install.md:3:This plugin installs [agentsh](https://www.agentsh.org/) -- a sandbox and policy enforcement layer that intercepts every shell command, file operation, and network call made by AI agents. After installation, all Bash tool calls in your harness session route through agentsh automatically via hooks.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/a5c/marketplace/plugins/agentsh/install.md:573:babysitter plugin:update-registry --plugin-name agentsh --plugin-version 1.0.0 --marketplace-name marketplace --project --json
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/a5c/marketplace/plugins/agentsh/uninstall.md:69:babysitter plugin:remove-from-registry --plugin-name agentsh --project --json
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/a5c/marketplace/plugins/dev-browser/install.md:3:This plugin installs [dev-browser](https://github.com/SawyerHood/dev-browser) -- a sandboxed browser automation CLI for AI agents. Scripts run in a QuickJS WASM sandbox with no host access, supporting persistent named pages, full Playwright API, screenshots, and AI-optimized page snapshots.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/a5c/marketplace/plugins/dev-browser/install.md:202:babysitter plugin:update-registry --plugin-name dev-browser --plugin-version 1.0.0 --marketplace-name marketplace --project --json
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/a5c/marketplace/plugins/qmd/install.md:3:This plugin installs [qmd](https://github.com/tobi/qmd) -- an on-device hybrid search engine for markdown files. It combines BM25 full-text, vector semantic search, and LLM re-ranking, all running locally via node-llama-cpp with GGUF models. After installation, your project's markdown files are indexed and searchable via MCP tools, CLI, or SDK.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/a5c/marketplace/plugins/qmd/install.md:277:babysitter plugin:update-registry --plugin-name qmd --plugin-version 1.0.0 --marketplace-name marketplace --project --json

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/a5c/marketplace/plugins/claude-mem/install.md:3:This plugin installs [claude-mem](https://github.com/thedotmack/claude-mem) -- persistent memory for Claude Code sessions. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/a5c/marketplace/plugins/claude-mem/install.md:13:claude plugin install claude-mem
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/a5c/marketplace/plugins/claude-mem/install.md:16:This installs the full plugin including lifecycle hooks, worker service, and MCP tools.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/AGENTS.md:3:This file governs agent behavior when the babysitter-pi plugin is active in an oh-my-pi session. Babysitter is the orchestration layer -- it drives multi-step workflows through process definitions, effects, and an iteration loop.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/AGENTS.md:16:You do not need to initialize babysitter manually -- the plugin handles it.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/AGENTS.md:47:The plugin's loop-driver extension controls iteration flow. Complete one task, post the result, and the driver handles the rest.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/AGENTS.md:69:After executing an effect, hand the outcome back to the Babysitter plugin/runtime bridge so the run state, journal, and pending-effect cache stay consistent.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/AGENTS.md:72:- Use the plugin-owned Babysitter bridge/command flow rather than inventing an alternate posting path.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/README.md:89:SDK_VERSION=$(node -e "try{const fs=require('fs');const path=require('path');const pluginRoot=process.env.PI_PLUGIN_ROOT||process.env.PLUGIN_ROOT||process.cwd();const probes=[path.join(pluginRoot,'versions.json'),path.join(pluginRoot,'plugins','babysitter-pi','versions.json'),path.join(pluginRoot,'node_modules','@a5c-ai','babysitter-pi','versions.json'),path.join(process.cwd(),'node_modules','@a5c-ai','babysitter-pi','versions.json')];for(const probe of probes){if(fs.existsSync(probe)){console.log(JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(probe,'utf8')).sdkVersion||'latest');process.exit(0)}}console.log('latest')}catch{console.log('latest')}")

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:2:description: manage babysitter plugins. use this command to see the list of installed babysitter plugins, their status, and manage them (install, update, uninstall, list from marketplace, add marketplace, configure plugin, create new plugin, etc).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:6:This command installs and manages plugins for babysitter. A plugin is a version-managed package of contextual instructions (for install, uninstall, configure, and update/migrate between versions), not a conventional software plugin.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:8:if the command is run without arguments, it lists all installed plugins with their name, version, marketplace, installation date, and last update date. as well as marketplaces added to the system. and instructions on how to install new plugins from marketplaces.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:11:babysitter plugin:add-marketplace --marketplace-url https://github.com/a5c-ai/babysitter --marketplace-path plugins/a5c/marketplace/marketplace.json --global --json
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:20:Marketplaces are git repositories containing a `marketplace.json` manifest and plugin package directories. The SDK clones them locally with `--depth 1`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:31:babysitter plugin:add-marketplace --marketplace-url <url> [--marketplace-path <relative-path>] [--marketplace-branch <ref>] [--force] --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:39:babysitter plugin:update-marketplace --marketplace-name <name> [--marketplace-branch <ref>] --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:44:### Listing plugins in a marketplace
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:47:babysitter plugin:list-plugins --marketplace-name <name> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:50:Reads the `marketplace.json` manifest and returns all available plugins sorted alphabetically by name. Each entry includes: name, description, latestVersion, versions array, packagePath, tags, and author.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:54:**Note:** For `plugin:install`, `plugin:update`, `plugin:configure`, and `plugin:list-plugins`, the `--marketplace-name` flag is auto-detected when only one marketplace is cloned for the given scope. You can omit it if there's only one marketplace.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:58:1. Update the marketplace: `babysitter plugin:update-marketplace --marketplace-name <name> --global|--project`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:59:2. Check current state: `babysitter plugin:list-installed --global|--project` to see installed plugins and versions
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:60:3. Install the plugin:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:63:babysitter plugin:install --plugin-name <name> [--marketplace-name <mp>] --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:66:This command resolves the plugin package path from the marketplace manifest, reads `install.md` from the plugin package directory, and returns the installation instructions. If an `install-process.js` file exists, the instructions may reference it as an automated install process.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:72:babysitter plugin:update-registry --plugin-name <name> --plugin-version <ver> --marketplace-name <mp> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:78:babysitter plugin:update --plugin-name <name> --marketplace-name <mp> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:84:3. Looks in the plugin package's `migrations/` directory for migration files
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:96:babysitter plugin:update-registry --plugin-name <name> --plugin-version <new-ver> --marketplace-name <mp> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:102:babysitter plugin:uninstall --plugin-name <name> --marketplace-name <mp> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:105:Reads `uninstall.md` from the plugin package directory and returns the uninstall instructions. After performing the uninstall steps, remove from registry:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:108:babysitter plugin:remove-from-registry --plugin-name <name> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:114:babysitter plugin:configure --plugin-name <name> --marketplace-name <mp> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:117:Reads `configure.md` from the plugin package directory and returns configuration instructions.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:121:The plugin registry (`plugin-registry.json`) tracks installed plugins with schema version `2026.01.plugin-registry-v1`. Writes use atomic file operations (temp + rename) for crash safety.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:124:- Global: `~/.a5c/plugin-registry.json`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:125:- Project: `<projectDir>/.a5c/plugin-registry.json`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:127:### List installed plugins
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:130:babysitter plugin:list-installed --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:133:Returns all installed plugins sorted alphabetically. In `--json` mode, returns an array of registry entries. In human mode, displays a formatted table with name, version, marketplace, and timestamps.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:138:babysitter plugin:remove-from-registry --plugin-name <name> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:141:Removes a plugin entry from the registry. Returns error if the plugin is not present.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:145:To create a new plugin package from scratch, use the `meta/plugin-creation` babysitter process. This process guides you through requirements analysis, structure design, instruction authoring, optional process file generation, validation, and marketplace integration.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:147:### Using the plugin creation process
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:149:Orchestrate a babysitter run with the plugin creation process:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:153:cat > /tmp/plugin-inputs.json << 'EOF'
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:155:  "pluginName": "my-plugin",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:156:  "description": "What the plugin does — be specific about install/configure/uninstall behavior",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:158:  "outputDir": "./plugins",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:176:  --process-id meta/plugin-creation \
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:177:  --entry library/specializations/meta/plugin-creation.js#process \
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:178:  --inputs /tmp/plugin-inputs.json \
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:179:  --prompt "Create a new babysitter plugin package" \
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:185:The process creates a complete plugin package directory:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:194:| `process/main.js` | *(optional)* Main process the plugin contributes |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:199:1. **Requirements Analysis** — Analyzes plugin purpose, prerequisites, config options, file structure
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:208:For simple plugins that only need instruction files, you can create the package manually following the structure below and the [Plugin Author Guide](docs/plugins/plugin-author-guide.md).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:213:my-plugin/
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:214:  package.json         # Optional (name field used as plugin ID, falls back to directory name)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:234:| `plugin:add-marketplace` | `--marketplace-url`, `--global\|--project` [`--marketplace-path`, `--marketplace-branch`, `--force`] | Clone a marketplace repository |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:235:| `plugin:update-marketplace` | `--marketplace-name`, `--global\|--project` [`--marketplace-branch`] | Pull latest marketplace changes (optionally switch branch) |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:236:| `plugin:list-plugins` | `--marketplace-name`, `--global\|--project` | List available plugins in a marketplace |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:237:| `plugin:install` | `--plugin-name`, `--marketplace-name`, `--global\|--project` | Get install instructions for a plugin |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:238:| `plugin:uninstall` | `--plugin-name`, `--marketplace-name`, `--global\|--project` | Get uninstall instructions for a plugin |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:239:| `plugin:update` | `--plugin-name`, `--marketplace-name`, `--global\|--project` | Resolve migration chain and get update instructions |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:240:| `plugin:configure` | `--plugin-name`, `--marketplace-name`, `--global\|--project` | Get configuration instructions for a plugin |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:241:| `plugin:list-installed` | `--global\|--project` | List all installed plugins |

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:243:| `plugin:remove-from-registry` | `--plugin-name`, `--global\|--project` | Remove a plugin entry from the registry |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:247:After working with plugins, prompt the user to share improvements or report issues:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:249:- **Improved a plugin's install/configure/uninstall instructions**: `/babysitter:contrib library contribution: improved [plugin-name] [install|configure|uninstall] instructions`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:250:- **Created a new plugin**: `/babysitter:contrib library contribution: new plugin [plugin-name] -- [description]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:251:- **Found a bug in a plugin or the plugin system**: `/babysitter:contrib bug report: [description, e.g. "plugin:update-registry fails when marketplace hasn't been cloned"]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:252:- **Plugin install/configure instructions were confusing or wrong**: `/babysitter:contrib bug report: [plugin-name] install instructions [description of what was wrong]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:253:- **Have an idea for a new plugin**: `/babysitter:contrib feature request: plugin idea -- [description]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter/commands/plugins.md:255:Even reporting that a plugin's instructions were unclear helps improve it for the next user.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/a5c/marketplace/plugins/basic-security/uninstall.md:70:- The `no-eval`, `no-implied-eval`, `no-new-func`, `no-script-url` rules (only if they were added by this plugin — check git history)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/a5c/marketplace/plugins/basic-security/uninstall.md:72:- Any React security rules (`react/no-danger`, `react/no-danger-with-children`) added by this plugin
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/a5c/marketplace/plugins/basic-security/uninstall.md:84:Edit `.golangci.yml` and remove `gosec`, `bodyclose`, `sqlclosecheck`, `noctx` from the enabled linters list (only if added by this plugin).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/a5c/marketplace/plugins/basic-security/uninstall.md:98:### If husky was installed only for this plugin (no testing-suite):
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/a5c/marketplace/plugins/basic-security/uninstall.md:105:### If pre-commit framework was installed only for this plugin:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/a5c/marketplace/plugins/basic-security/uninstall.md:122:Edit `.gitignore` and remove the security-related patterns that were added during install (the block with `.env`, `*.pem`, `*.key`, etc.). .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/a5c/marketplace/plugins/basic-security/uninstall.md:128:Deregister the plugin from the project-level plugin registry:

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/library/specializations/technical-documentation/agents/docs-platform-engineer/AGENT.md:376:plugins: [
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/library/specializations/technical-documentation/agents/docs-platform-engineer/AGENT.md:378:    '@docusaurus/plugin-ideal-image',
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:2:name: plugins
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:3:description: manage babysitter plugins. use this command to see the list of installed babysitter plugins, their status, and manage them (install, update, uninstall, list from marketplace, add marketplace, configure plugin, create new plugin, etc).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:6:# plugins
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:8:This command installs and manages plugins for babysitter. A plugin is a version-managed package of contextual instructions (for install, uninstall, configure, and update/migrate between versions), not a conventional software plugin.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:10:if the command is run without arguments, it lists all installed plugins with their name, version, marketplace, installation date, and last update date. as well as marketplaces added to the system. and instructions on how to install new plugins from marketplaces.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:13:babysitter plugin:add-marketplace --marketplace-url https://github.com/a5c-ai/babysitter --marketplace-path plugins/a5c/marketplace/marketplace.json --global --json
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:22:Marketplaces are git repositories containing a `marketplace.json` manifest and plugin package directories. The SDK clones them locally with `--depth 1`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:33:babysitter plugin:add-marketplace --marketplace-url <url> [--marketplace-path <relative-path>] [--marketplace-branch <ref>] [--force] --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:41:babysitter plugin:update-marketplace --marketplace-name <name> [--marketplace-branch <ref>] --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:46:### Listing plugins in a marketplace
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:49:babysitter plugin:list-plugins --marketplace-name <name> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:52:Reads the `marketplace.json` manifest and returns all available plugins sorted alphabetically by name. Each entry includes: name, description, latestVersion, versions array, packagePath, tags, and author.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:56:**Note:** For `plugin:install`, `plugin:update`, `plugin:configure`, and `plugin:list-plugins`, the `--marketplace-name` flag is auto-detected when only one marketplace is cloned for the given scope. You can omit it if there's only one marketplace.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:60:1. Update the marketplace: `babysitter plugin:update-marketplace --marketplace-name <name> --global|--project`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:61:2. Check current state: `babysitter plugin:list-installed --global|--project` to see installed plugins and versions
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:62:3. Install the plugin:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:65:babysitter plugin:install --plugin-name <name> [--marketplace-name <mp>] --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:68:This command resolves the plugin package path from the marketplace manifest, reads `install.md` from the plugin package directory, and returns the installation instructions. If an `install-process.js` file exists, the instructions may reference it as an automated install process.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:74:babysitter plugin:update-registry --plugin-name <name> --plugin-version <ver> --marketplace-name <mp> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:80:babysitter plugin:update --plugin-name <name> --marketplace-name <mp> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:86:3. Looks in the plugin package's `migrations/` directory for migration files
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:98:babysitter plugin:update-registry --plugin-name <name> --plugin-version <new-ver> --marketplace-name <mp> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:104:babysitter plugin:uninstall --plugin-name <name> --marketplace-name <mp> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:107:Reads `uninstall.md` from the plugin package directory and returns the uninstall instructions. After performing the uninstall steps, remove from registry:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:110:babysitter plugin:remove-from-registry --plugin-name <name> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:116:babysitter plugin:configure --plugin-name <name> --marketplace-name <mp> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:119:Reads `configure.md` from the plugin package directory and returns configuration instructions.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:123:The plugin registry (`plugin-registry.json`) tracks installed plugins with schema version `2026.01.plugin-registry-v1`. Writes use atomic file operations (temp + rename) for crash safety.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:126:- Global: `~/.a5c/plugin-registry.json`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:127:- Project: `<projectDir>/.a5c/plugin-registry.json`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:129:### List installed plugins
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:132:babysitter plugin:list-installed --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:135:Returns all installed plugins sorted alphabetically. In `--json` mode, returns an array of registry entries. In human mode, displays a formatted table with name, version, marketplace, and timestamps.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:140:babysitter plugin:remove-from-registry --plugin-name <name> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:143:Removes a plugin entry from the registry. Returns error if the plugin is not present.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:147:To create a new plugin package from scratch, use the `meta/plugin-creation` babysitter process. This process guides you through requirements analysis, structure design, instruction authoring, optional process file generation, validation, and marketplace integration.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:149:### Using the plugin creation process
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:151:Orchestrate a babysitter run with the plugin creation process:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:155:cat > /tmp/plugin-inputs.json << 'EOF'
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:157:  "pluginName": "my-plugin",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:158:  "description": "What the plugin does — be specific about install/configure/uninstall behavior",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:160:  "outputDir": "./plugins",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:178:  --process-id meta/plugin-creation \
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:179:  --entry library/specializations/meta/plugin-creation.js#process \
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:180:  --inputs /tmp/plugin-inputs.json \
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:181:  --prompt "Create a new babysitter plugin package" \
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:187:The process creates a complete plugin package directory:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:196:| `process/main.js` | *(optional)* Main process the plugin contributes |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:201:1. **Requirements Analysis** — Analyzes plugin purpose, prerequisites, config options, file structure
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:210:For simple plugins that only need instruction files, you can create the package manually following the structure below and the [Plugin Author Guide](docs/plugins/plugin-author-guide.md).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:215:my-plugin/
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:216:  package.json         # Optional (name field used as plugin ID, falls back to directory name)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:236:| `plugin:add-marketplace` | `--marketplace-url`, `--global\|--project` [`--marketplace-path`, `--marketplace-branch`, `--force`] | Clone a marketplace repository |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:237:| `plugin:update-marketplace` | `--marketplace-name`, `--global\|--project` [`--marketplace-branch`] | Pull latest marketplace changes (optionally switch branch) |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:238:| `plugin:list-plugins` | `--marketplace-name`, `--global\|--project` | List available plugins in a marketplace |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:239:| `plugin:install` | `--plugin-name`, `--marketplace-name`, `--global\|--project` | Get install instructions for a plugin |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:240:| `plugin:uninstall` | `--plugin-name`, `--marketplace-name`, `--global\|--project` | Get uninstall instructions for a plugin |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:241:| `plugin:update` | `--plugin-name`, `--marketplace-name`, `--global\|--project` | Resolve migration chain and get update instructions |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:242:| `plugin:configure` | `--plugin-name`, `--marketplace-name`, `--global\|--project` | Get configuration instructions for a plugin |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:243:| `plugin:list-installed` | `--global\|--project` | List all installed plugins |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:244:| `plugin:update-registry` | `--plugin-name`, `--plugin-version`, `--marketplace-name`, `--global\|--project` | Register or update a plugin entry |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:245:| `plugin:remove-from-registry` | `--plugin-name`, `--global\|--project` | Remove a plugin entry from the registry |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:249:After working with plugins, prompt the user to share improvements or report issues:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:251:- **Improved a plugin's install/configure/uninstall instructions**: `/babysitter:contrib library contribution: improved [plugin-name] [install|configure|uninstall] instructions`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:252:- **Created a new plugin**: `/babysitter:contrib library contribution: new plugin [plugin-name] -- [description]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:253:- **Found a bug in a plugin or the plugin system**: `/babysitter:contrib bug report: [description, e.g. "plugin:update-registry fails when marketplace hasn't been cloned"]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:254:- **Plugin install/configure instructions were confusing or wrong**: `/babysitter:contrib bug report: [plugin-name] install instructions [description of what was wrong]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:255:- **Have an idea for a new plugin**: `/babysitter:contrib feature request: plugin idea -- [description]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:257:Even reporting that a plugin's instructions were unclear helps improve it for the next user.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/library/specializations/domains/social-sciences-humanities/arts-culture/film-tv-production/full-production.js:134:    question: `Phase 2 Complete: ${characterResult.characters?.supporting?.length + 2 || 0} characters profiled with relationships and visual designs. Ready to proceed to world building?`,
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/library/specializations/domains/social-sciences-humanities/arts-culture/film-tv-production/full-production.js:140:        protagonist: characterResult.characters?.protagonist?.profile?.name,
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/library/specializations/domains/social-sciences-humanities/arts-culture/film-tv-production/full-production.js:517:      task: 'Execute complete character creation with profiles, relationships, and visual designs',

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:6:This command installs and manages plugins for babysitter. A plugin is a version-managed package of contextual instructions (for install, uninstall, configure, and update/migrate between versions), not a conventional software plugin.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:8:if the command is run without arguments, it lists all installed plugins with their name, version, marketplace, installation date, and last update date. as well as marketplaces added to the system. and instructions on how to install new plugins from marketplaces.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:11:babysitter plugin:add-marketplace --marketplace-url https://github.com/a5c-ai/babysitter --marketplace-path plugins/a5c/marketplace/marketplace.json --global --json
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:20:Marketplaces are git repositories containing a `marketplace.json` manifest and plugin package directories. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:31:babysitter plugin:add-marketplace --marketplace-url <url> [--marketplace-path <relative-path>] [--marketplace-branch <ref>] [--force] --global|--project [--json]

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:47:babysitter plugin:list-plugins --marketplace-name <name> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:50:Reads the `marketplace.json` manifest and returns all available plugins sorted alphabetically by name. Each entry includes: name, description, latestVersion, versions array, packagePath, tags, and author.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:54:**Note:** For `plugin:install`, `plugin:update`, `plugin:configure`, and `plugin:list-plugins`, the `--marketplace-name` flag is auto-detected when only one marketplace is cloned for the given scope. You can omit it if there's only one marketplace.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:58:1. Update the marketplace: `babysitter plugin:update-marketplace --marketplace-name <name> --global|--project`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:59:2. Check current state: `babysitter plugin:list-installed --global|--project` to see installed plugins and versions

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:66:This command resolves the plugin package path from the marketplace manifest, reads `install.md` from the plugin package directory, and returns the installation instructions. If an `install-process.js` file exists, the instructions may reference it as an automated install process.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:72:babysitter plugin:update-registry --plugin-name <name> --plugin-version <ver> --marketplace-name <mp> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:78:babysitter plugin:update --plugin-name <name> --marketplace-name <mp> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:84:3. Looks in the plugin package's `migrations/` directory for migration files

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:108:babysitter plugin:remove-from-registry --plugin-name <name> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:114:babysitter plugin:configure --plugin-name <name> --marketplace-name <mp> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:117:Reads `configure.md` from the plugin package directory and returns configuration instructions.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:121:The plugin registry (`plugin-registry.json`) tracks installed plugins with schema version `2026.01.plugin-registry-v1`. Writes use atomic file operations (temp + rename) for crash safety.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:124:- Global: `~/.a5c/plugin-registry.json`

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:133:Returns all installed plugins sorted alphabetically. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:138:babysitter plugin:remove-from-registry --plugin-name <name> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:141:Removes a plugin entry from the registry. Returns error if the plugin is not present.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:145:To create a new plugin package from scratch, use the `meta/plugin-creation` babysitter process. This process guides you through requirements analysis, structure design, instruction authoring, optional process file generation, validation, and marketplace integration.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:147:### Using the plugin creation process

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:249:- **Improved a plugin's install/configure/uninstall instructions**: `/babysitter:contrib library contribution: improved [plugin-name] [install|configure|uninstall] instructions`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:250:- **Created a new plugin**: `/babysitter:contrib library contribution: new plugin [plugin-name] -- [description]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:251:- **Found a bug in a plugin or the plugin system**: `/babysitter:contrib bug report: [description, e.g. "plugin:update-registry fails when marketplace hasn't been cloned"]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/commands/plugins.md:252:- **Plugin install/configure instructions were confusing or wrong**: `/babysitter:contrib bug report: [plugin-name] install instructions [description of what was wrong]`

Babysitter is the orchestration layer -- it drives multi-step workflows through process definitions, effects, and an iteration loop.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/AGENTS.md:16:You do not need to initialize babysitter manually -- the plugin handles it.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/AGENTS.md:47:The plugin's loop-driver extension controls iteration flow. Complete one task, post the result, and the driver handles the rest.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/AGENTS.md:69:After executing an effect, hand the outcome back to the Babysitter plugin/runtime bridge so the run state, journal, and pending-effect cache stay consistent.
