.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:2:description: manage babysitter plugins. use this command to see the list of installed babysitter plugins, their status, and manage them (install, update, uninstall, list from marketplace, add marketplace, configure plugin, create new plugin, etc).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:6:This command installs and manages plugins for babysitter. A plugin is a version-managed package of contextual instructions (for install, uninstall, configure, and update/migrate between versions), not a conventional software plugin.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:8:if the command is run without arguments, it lists all installed plugins with their name, version, marketplace, installation date, and last update date. as well as marketplaces added to the system. and instructions on how to install new plugins from marketplaces.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:11:babysitter plugin:add-marketplace --marketplace-url https://github.com/a5c-ai/babysitter --marketplace-path plugins/a5c/marketplace/marketplace.json --global --json
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:20:Marketplaces are git repositories containing a `marketplace.json` manifest and plugin package directories. The SDK clones them locally with `--depth 1`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:31:babysitter plugin:add-marketplace --marketplace-url <url> [--marketplace-path <relative-path>] [--marketplace-branch <ref>] [--force] --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:39:babysitter plugin:update-marketplace --marketplace-name <name> [--marketplace-branch <ref>] --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:44:### Listing plugins in a marketplace
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:47:babysitter plugin:list-plugins --marketplace-name <name> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:50:Reads the `marketplace.json` manifest and returns all available plugins sorted alphabetically by name. Each entry includes: name, description, latestVersion, versions array, packagePath, tags, and author.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:54:**Note:** For `plugin:install`, `plugin:update`, `plugin:configure`, and `plugin:list-plugins`, the `--marketplace-name` flag is auto-detected when only one marketplace is cloned for the given scope. You can omit it if there's only one marketplace.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:58:1. Update the marketplace: `babysitter plugin:update-marketplace --marketplace-name <name> --global|--project`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:59:2. Check current state: `babysitter plugin:list-installed --global|--project` to see installed plugins and versions
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:60:3. Install the plugin:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:63:babysitter plugin:install --plugin-name <name> [--marketplace-name <mp>] --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:66:This command resolves the plugin package path from the marketplace manifest, reads `install.md` from the plugin package directory, and returns the installation instructions. If an `install-process.js` file exists, the instructions may reference it as an automated install process.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:72:babysitter plugin:update-registry --plugin-name <name> --plugin-version <ver> --marketplace-name <mp> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:78:babysitter plugin:update --plugin-name <name> --marketplace-name <mp> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:84:3. Looks in the plugin package's `migrations/` directory for migration files
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:96:babysitter plugin:update-registry --plugin-name <name> --plugin-version <new-ver> --marketplace-name <mp> --global|--project [--json]

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:105:Reads `uninstall.md` from the plugin package directory and returns the uninstall instructions. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:108:babysitter plugin:remove-from-registry --plugin-name <name> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:114:babysitter plugin:configure --plugin-name <name> --marketplace-name <mp> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:117:Reads `configure.md` from the plugin package directory and returns configuration instructions.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:121:The plugin registry (`plugin-registry.json`) tracks installed plugins with schema version `2026.01.plugin-registry-v1`. Writes use atomic file operations (temp + rename) for crash safety.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:124:- Global: `~/.a5c/plugin-registry.json`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:125:- Project: `<projectDir>/.a5c/plugin-registry.json`

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:133:Returns all installed plugins sorted alphabetically. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:138:babysitter plugin:remove-from-registry --plugin-name <name> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:141:Removes a plugin entry from the registry. Returns error if the plugin is not present.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:145:To create a new plugin package from scratch, use the `meta/plugin-creation` babysitter process. This process guides you through requirements analysis, structure design, instruction authoring, optional process file generation, validation, and marketplace integration.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:147:### Using the plugin creation process

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:249:- **Improved a plugin's install/configure/uninstall instructions**: `/babysitter:contrib library contribution: improved [plugin-name] [install|configure|uninstall] instructions`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:250:- **Created a new plugin**: `/babysitter:contrib library contribution: new plugin [plugin-name] -- [description]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:251:- **Found a bug in a plugin or the plugin system**: `/babysitter:contrib bug report: [description, e.g. "plugin:update-registry fails when marketplace hasn't been cloned"]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/commands/plugins.md:252:- **Plugin install/configure instructions were confusing or wrong**: `/babysitter:contrib bug report: [plugin-name] install instructions [description of what was wrong]`

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:3:description: manage babysitter plugins. use this command to see the list of installed babysitter plugins, their status, and manage them (install, update, uninstall, list from marketplace, add marketplace, configure plugin, create new plugin, etc).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:6:# plugins
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:8:This command installs and manages plugins for babysitter. A plugin is a version-managed package of contextual instructions (for install, uninstall, configure, and update/migrate between versions), not a conventional software plugin.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:10:if the command is run without arguments, it lists all installed plugins with their name, version, marketplace, installation date, and last update date. as well as marketplaces added to the system. and instructions on how to install new plugins from marketplaces.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:13:babysitter plugin:add-marketplace --marketplace-url https://github.com/a5c-ai/babysitter --marketplace-path plugins/a5c/marketplace/marketplace.json --global --json
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:22:Marketplaces are git repositories containing a `marketplace.json` manifest and plugin package directories. The SDK clones them locally with `--depth 1`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:33:babysitter plugin:add-marketplace --marketplace-url <url> [--marketplace-path <relative-path>] [--marketplace-branch <ref>] [--force] --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:41:babysitter plugin:update-marketplace --marketplace-name <name> [--marketplace-branch <ref>] --global|--project [--json]

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:49:babysitter plugin:list-plugins --marketplace-name <name> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:52:Reads the `marketplace.json` manifest and returns all available plugins sorted alphabetically by name. Each entry includes: name, description, latestVersion, versions array, packagePath, tags, and author.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:56:**Note:** For `plugin:install`, `plugin:update`, `plugin:configure`, and `plugin:list-plugins`, the `--marketplace-name` flag is auto-detected when only one marketplace is cloned for the given scope. You can omit it if there's only one marketplace.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:60:1. Update the marketplace: `babysitter plugin:update-marketplace --marketplace-name <name> --global|--project`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:61:2. Check current state: `babysitter plugin:list-installed --global|--project` to see installed plugins and versions
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:62:3. Install the plugin:

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:68:This command resolves the plugin package path from the marketplace manifest, reads `install.md` from the plugin package directory, and returns the installation instructions. If an `install-process.js` file exists, the instructions may reference it as an automated install process.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:74:babysitter plugin:update-registry --plugin-name <name> --plugin-version <ver> --marketplace-name <mp> --global|--project [--json]

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:86:3. Looks in the plugin package's `migrations/` directory for migration files

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:110:babysitter plugin:remove-from-registry --plugin-name <name> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:116:babysitter plugin:configure --plugin-name <name> --marketplace-name <mp> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:119:Reads `configure.md` from the plugin package directory and returns configuration instructions.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:123:The plugin registry (`plugin-registry.json`) tracks installed plugins with schema version `2026.01.plugin-registry-v1`. Writes use atomic file operations (temp + rename) for crash safety.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:126:- Global: `~/.a5c/plugin-registry.json`

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:129:### List installed plugins
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:132:babysitter plugin:list-installed --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:135:Returns all installed plugins sorted alphabetically. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:140:babysitter plugin:remove-from-registry --plugin-name <name> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:143:Removes a plugin entry from the registry. Returns error if the plugin is not present.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:147:To create a new plugin package from scratch, use the `meta/plugin-creation` babysitter process. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:251:- **Improved a plugin's install/configure/uninstall instructions**: `/babysitter:contrib library contribution: improved [plugin-name] [install|configure|uninstall] instructions`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:252:- **Created a new plugin**: `/babysitter:contrib library contribution: new plugin [plugin-name] -- [description]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:253:- **Found a bug in a plugin or the plugin system**: `/babysitter:contrib bug report: [description, e.g. "plugin:update-registry fails when marketplace hasn't been cloned"]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:254:- **Plugin install/configure instructions were confusing or wrong**: `/babysitter:contrib bug report: [plugin-name] install instructions [description of what was wrong]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-omp/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:255:- **Have an idea for a new plugin**: `/babysitter:contrib feature request: plugin idea -- [description]`

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:2:name: plugins
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:3:description: manage babysitter plugins. use this command to see the list of installed babysitter plugins, their status, and manage them (install, update, uninstall, list from marketplace, add marketplace, configure plugin, create new plugin, etc).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:6:# plugins
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:8:This command installs and manages plugins for babysitter. A plugin is a version-managed package of contextual instructions (for install, uninstall, configure, and update/migrate between versions), not a conventional software plugin.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:10:if the command is run without arguments, it lists all installed plugins with their name, version, marketplace, installation date, and last update date. as well as marketplaces added to the system. and instructions on how to install new plugins from marketplaces.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:13:babysitter plugin:add-marketplace --marketplace-url https://github.com/a5c-ai/babysitter --marketplace-path plugins/a5c/marketplace/marketplace.json --global --json
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:22:Marketplaces are git repositories containing a `marketplace.json` manifest and plugin package directories. The SDK clones them locally with `--depth 1`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:33:babysitter plugin:add-marketplace --marketplace-url <url> [--marketplace-path <relative-path>] [--marketplace-branch <ref>] [--force] --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:41:babysitter plugin:update-marketplace --marketplace-name <name> [--marketplace-branch <ref>] --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:46:### Listing plugins in a marketplace
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:49:babysitter plugin:list-plugins --marketplace-name <name> --global|--project [--json]
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:52:Reads the `marketplace.json` manifest and returns all available plugins sorted alphabetically by name. Each entry includes: name, description, latestVersion, versions array, packagePath, tags, and author.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:56:**Note:** For `plugin:install`, `plugin:update`, `plugin:configure`, and `plugin:list-plugins`, the `--marketplace-name` flag is auto-detected when only one marketplace is cloned for the given scope. You can omit it if there's only one marketplace.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:60:1. Update the marketplace: `babysitter plugin:update-marketplace --marketplace-name <name> --global|--project`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:61:2. Check current state: `babysitter plugin:list-installed --global|--project` to see installed plugins and versions
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:62:3. Install the plugin:

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:68:This command resolves the plugin package path from the marketplace manifest, reads `install.md` from the plugin package directory, and returns the installation instructions. If an `install-process.js` file exists, the instructions may reference it as an automated install process.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:74:babysitter plugin:update-registry --plugin-name <name> --plugin-version <ver> --marketplace-name <mp> --global|--project [--json]

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:86:3. Looks in the plugin package's `migrations/` directory for migration files

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:123:The plugin registry (`plugin-registry.json`) tracks installed plugins with schema version `2026.01.plugin-registry-v1`. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:126:- Global: `~/.a5c/plugin-registry.json`

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:140:babysitter plugin:remove-from-registry --plugin-name <name> --global|--project [--json]

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:251:- **Improved a plugin's install/configure/uninstall instructions**: `/babysitter:contrib library contribution: improved [plugin-name] [install|configure|uninstall] instructions`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:252:- **Created a new plugin**: `/babysitter:contrib library contribution: new plugin [plugin-name] -- [description]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:253:- **Found a bug in a plugin or the plugin system**: `/babysitter:contrib bug report: [description, e.g. "plugin:update-registry fails when marketplace hasn't been cloned"]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/skills/plugins/SKILL.md:254:- **Plugin install/configure instructions were confusing or wrong**: `/babysitter:contrib bug report: [plugin-name] install instructions [description of what was wrong]`

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/commands/plugins.md:2:description: manage babysitter plugins. use this command to see the list of installed babysitter plugins, their status, and manage them (install, update, uninstall, list from marketplace, add marketplace, configure plugin, create new plugin, etc).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/commands/plugins.md:6:This command installs and manages plugins for babysitter. A plugin is a version-managed package of contextual instructions (for install, uninstall, configure, and update/migrate between versions), not a conventional software plugin.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/commands/plugins.md:8:if the command is run without arguments, it lists all installed plugins with their name, version, marketplace, installation date, and last update date. as well as marketplaces added to the system. and instructions on how to install new plugins from marketplaces.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/commands/plugins.md:20:Marketplaces are git repositories containing a `marketplace.json` manifest and plugin package directories. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-opencode/commands/plugins.md:54:**Note:** For `plugin:install`, `plugin:update`, `plugin:configure`, and `plugin:list-plugins`, the `--marketplace-name` flag is auto-detected when only one marketplace is cloned for the given scope. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-github/commands/plugins.md:2:description: manage babysitter plugins. use this command to see the list of installed babysitter plugins, their status, and manage them (install, update, uninstall, list from marketplace, add marketplace, configure plugin, create new plugin, etc).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-github/commands/plugins.md:6:This command installs and manages plugins for babysitter. A plugin is a version-managed package of contextual instructions (for install, uninstall, configure, and update/migrate between versions), not a conventional software plugin.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-github/commands/plugins.md:8:if the command is run without arguments, it lists all installed plugins with their name, version, marketplace, installation date, and last update date. as well as marketplaces added to the system. and instructions on how to install new plugins from marketplaces.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-github/commands/plugins.md:20:Marketplaces are git repositories containing a `marketplace.json` manifest and plugin package directories. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-github/commands/plugins.md:247:After working with plugins, prompt the user to share improvements or report issues:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-github/commands/plugins.md:249:- **Improved a plugin's install/configure/uninstall instructions**: `/babysitter:contrib library contribution: improved [plugin-name] [install|configure|uninstall] instructions`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-github/commands/plugins.md:250:- **Created a new plugin**: `/babysitter:contrib library contribution: new plugin [plugin-name] -- [description]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-github/commands/plugins.md:251:- **Found a bug in a plugin or the plugin system**: `/babysitter:contrib bug report: [description, e.g. "plugin:update-registry fails when marketplace hasn't been cloned"]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-github/commands/plugins.md:252:- **Plugin install/configure instructions were confusing or wrong**: `/babysitter:contrib bug report: [plugin-name] install instructions [description of what was wrong]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-github/commands/plugins.md:253:- **Have an idea for a new plugin**: `/babysitter:contrib feature request: plugin idea -- [description]`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-github/commands/plugins.md:255:Even reporting that a plugin's instructions were unclear helps improve it for the next user.

codex
A useful contrast is emerging already: Mnemosyne’s wrapper has no profile-aware behavior at all, while Public’s runtime and MCP stack explicitly expose layered tools, response profiles, and session-recovery surfaces. I’m grabbing the exact schema and config lines next so I can anchor that difference with precise evidence instead of hand-waving it.

For session recovery, use mode: \'resume\' with profile: \'resume\'. Modes: auto (default), quick (trigger-based), deep (comprehensive), focused (intent-optimized), resume (session recovery). Token Budget: 3500. # Iteration 003: TOOL/API SURFACE

## Focus
TOOL/API SURFACE: Analyze MCP tools, CLI commands, API endpoints, tool registration, profile separation.

## Findings
### Finding 1: Mnemosyne exposes a deliberately tiny plugin surface, not a full memory platform API
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts#L92](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L92), [external/package.json#L38](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/package.json#L38), [review-fixes.vitest.ts#L115](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/review-fixes.vitest.ts#L115)
- **Evidence type**: wrapper-confirmed
- **What it does**: the external repo returns one OpenCode plugin object with exactly five tools plus one compaction hook; there is no HTTP surface, no MCP server, and no secondary registration layer. By contrast, Public’s memory server test suite locks `TOOL_DEFINITIONS.length` at 43.
- **Why it matters for us**: Mnemosyne’s value on the API side is ergonomic minimalism, not capability breadth. It is a thin agent-facing facade over a backend binary, whereas Public already exposes a broad operational surface.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: The CLI bridge is safely implemented, but operationally under-instrumented
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts#L35](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L35), [external/src/index.ts#L42](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L42), [external/src/index.ts#L59](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L59), [external/src/index.ts#L72](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L72), [tool-schemas.ts#L699](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L699), [tool-schemas.ts#L755](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L755)
- **Evidence type**: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne shells out with `Bun.spawn(["mnemosyne", ...args])`, avoids shell interpolation, captures both stdout and stderr, and returns a friendly missing-binary message. But the wrapper only exposes `init`, `search`, `add`, and `delete`; it has no health, status, schema, or startup-readiness tool.
- **Why it matters for us**: the argv-array bridge is worth copying if we ever front an external memory binary, but the missing operational tools would be a regression versus Public’s `ccc_status`, `session_bootstrap`, and richer MCP diagnostics.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 3: Mnemosyne’s project/global split is ergonomic, but the actual scope key is weak
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts#L27](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L27), [external/src/index.ts#L30](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L30), [external/src/index.ts#L78](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L78), [external/src/index.ts#L171](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L171), [tool-schemas.ts#L43](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L43), [tool-schemas.ts#L69](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L69)
- **Evidence type**: both
- **What it does**: the wrapper derives project scope from the current directory basename, special-cases `global` to `default`, auto-inits the project collection on load, and lazily inits global on first global write. Public instead exposes explicit `specFolder`, `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, `sharedSpaceId`, and `sessionId` scope parameters.
- **Why it matters for us**: the two-surface ergonomics are attractive, but basename-derived identity is collision-prone and too coarse for Public’s governed and session-aware memory model.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 4: Mnemosyne has no profile separation; Public does
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts#L96](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L96), [external/src/index.ts#L210](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L210), [tool-schemas.ts#L44](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L44), [memory-context.ts#L1318](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts#L1318), [config.toml#L48](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.codex/config.toml#L48), [config.toml#L68](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.codex/config.toml#L68)
- **Evidence type**: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne offers one fixed tool family and one fixed compaction prompt block. Public supports response `profile` values on `memory_context`, intent-to-profile auto-routing, and runtime-level profile separation in `.codex/config.toml` (`context`, `research`, `review`, `write`, etc.).
- **Why it matters for us**: Mnemosyne’s simplicity is nice for adoption, but it cannot express Public’s profile-aware recovery and formatting contracts. Adopting it as-is would flatten a capability Public already uses for routing, safety, and UX.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 5: The compaction hook is the most transferable idea, but Public’s implementation substrate is stronger
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts#L208](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L208), [external/src/index.ts#L216](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L216), [spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L396](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L396), [spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L411](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L411), [spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L296](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L296)
- **Evidence type**: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne injects a static memory-usage checklist into `experimental.session.compacting`. Public’s compact-code-graph plugin already does compaction injection too, but through a cached bridge transport plan, with dedupe checks and a plugin status tool.
- **Why it matters for us**: the novel reusable part is not “compaction hook exists” but “memory-tool reminders survive compaction directly in prompt text.” Public can add that guidance inside its existing transport-plan architecture instead of copying Mnemosyne’s thinner standalone pattern.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 6: Mnemosyne’s hybrid retrieval and offline-first story are mostly backend contract, not wrapper logic
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts#L104](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L104), [external/README.md#L82](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L82), [external/README.md#L84](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L84), [external/README.md#L18](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L18), [memory-search.ts#L482](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L482), [context-server.ts#L759](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L759), [mcp-coco-index/README.md#L44](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md#L44)
- **Evidence type**: both
- **What it does**: the wrapper only quotes the query and calls `mnemosyne search`; BM25, sqlite-vec, RRF, ONNX local inference, and the `snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5` choice are README-level backend claims. Public already has hybrid `memory_search`, session-aware routing, and separately positions CocoIndex as semantic code search via CLI plus one MCP tool.
- **Why it matters for us**: there is no evidence here for a superior retrieval algorithm at the wrapper layer. The actionable delta is surface design and first-run UX, not replacing Public’s retrieval core with Mnemosyne’s unseen backend.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.84
- Questions addressed: tool registration, CLI bridge semantics, search/store/delete verbs, project vs global scoping, compaction prompt injection, profile separation, offline-first operational surface, Public overlap boundaries
- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s checked-out repo proves a thin OpenCode plugin facade over a CLI binary; the real hybrid-search mechanics remain README-level; Public already exceeds it on MCP breadth, layered routing, and profile-aware recovery; the strongest reusable idea is direct memory-tool guidance during compaction, not the backend contract itself; the strict validation command was run and reported `RESULT: PASSED` despite a read-only temp-file warning from the validator shell

## Reflection
- What worked: reading `src/index.ts` first made the boundary obvious very quickly, and comparing it directly with Public’s tool schemas and compaction plugin prevented stale “Public lacks hybrid retrieval” conclusions
- What did not work: the external repo does not include Mnemosyne backend internals, so BM25/vector/RRF remain documented claims only; this sandbox is read-only, so I could not update the phase artifacts on disk during this iteration

## Recommended Next Focus
LIFECYCLE + MUTATION MODEL: trace how Mnemosyne document IDs flow from recall to delete, compare overwrite/delete expectations with Public’s lineage/checkpoint surfaces, and define the minimum health/readiness contract a binary-backed memory facade would need before it is safe to prototype.

# Iteration 003: TOOL/API SURFACE

## Focus
TOOL/API SURFACE: Analyze MCP tools, CLI commands, API endpoints, tool registration, profile separation.

## Findings
### Finding 1: Mnemosyne exposes a deliberately tiny plugin surface, not a full memory platform API
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts#L92](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L92), [external/package.json#L38](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/package.json#L38), [review-fixes.vitest.ts#L115](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/review-fixes.vitest.ts#L115)
- **Evidence type**: wrapper-confirmed
- **What it does**: the external repo returns one OpenCode plugin object with exactly five tools plus one compaction hook; there is no HTTP surface, no MCP server, and no secondary registration layer. By contrast, Public’s memory server test suite locks `TOOL_DEFINITIONS.length` at 43.
- **Why it matters for us**: Mnemosyne’s value on the API side is ergonomic minimalism, not capability breadth. It is a thin agent-facing facade over a backend binary, whereas Public already exposes a broad operational surface.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: The CLI bridge is safely implemented, but operationally under-instrumented
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts#L35](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L35), [external/src/index.ts#L42](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L42), [external/src/index.ts#L59](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L59), [external/src/index.ts#L72](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L72), [tool-schemas.ts#L699](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L699), [tool-schemas.ts#L755](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L755)
- **Evidence type**: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne shells out with `Bun.spawn(["mnemosyne", ...args])`, avoids shell interpolation, captures both stdout and stderr, and returns a friendly missing-binary message. But the wrapper only exposes `init`, `search`, `add`, and `delete`; it has no health, status, schema, or startup-readiness tool.
- **Why it matters for us**: the argv-array bridge is worth copying if we ever front an external memory binary, but the missing operational tools would be a regression versus Public’s `ccc_status`, `session_bootstrap`, and richer MCP diagnostics.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

- What did not work: the external repo does not include Mnemosyne backend internals, so BM25/vector/RRF remain documented claims only; this sandbox is read-only, so I could not update the phase artifacts on disk during this iteration

## Recommended Next Focus
LIFECYCLE + MUTATION MODEL: trace how Mnemosyne document IDs flow from recall to delete, compare overwrite/delete expectations with Public’s lineage/checkpoint surfaces, and define the minimum health/readiness contract a binary-backed memory facade would need before it is safe to prototype.
